column7: I like that NetSuite tracks all of our transactions and products sold. I've learned to create scripts that allow me to get dashboards and data out of NetSuite. Using cloud features has been the most helpful because it lets me see under the hood. Once we went live and started working with ACS, the internal NetSuite consulting team, our experience improved significantly.
column8: As a startup, if you add multiple modules that you do not end up using in the future, there's no way to remove them. If you remove them, you cannot lower the contract value. So I would start with what you actually need and then add on as you go. Don't buy into the bundle because if you don't need something in the future, you will be stuck with it forever, and the value of the contract will never go down. The initial setup was very difficult. We hired a consulting firm to do it for us, and they were  terrible, which made the experience unpleasant. We paid for a whole year before we even went live. It's a very expensive product to go an entire year without using it.

column7: NetSuite has been an invaluable platform for centralizing our business operations across inventory, accounting, and reporting. What stands out most is the depth and accuracy of the real-time reporting, which gives us a complete financial picture at any given moment without having to pull data from multiple disconnected systems. The inventory management capabilities are particularly strong, allowing us to track stock levels, manage purchase orders, and forecast demand in a way that keeps our operations running efficiently. Having accounting, inventory, and reporting all living in one platform has eliminated a significant amount of manual reconciliation and data entry that used to create errors and slow down our close process. The customization options for dashboards and reports also mean we can surface exactly the metrics that matter most to our team without wading through irrelevant data. For a business operating across ecommerce and multiple channels, having NetSuite as the financial and operational backbone gives us the confidence that our numbers are accurate and up to date.
column8: NetSuite's biggest drawback is the complexity and cost of implementation and customization. Out of the box it requires significant configuration to fit your specific business needs, and that almost always means relying on either internal technical resources or expensive outside consultants to get it set up properly. The user interface, while functional, feels dated compared to more modern platforms and can be unintuitive for new users, which makes onboarding and training more time consuming than it should be. Reporting is powerful but building custom reports can be cumbersome and often requires more technical knowledge than the average user has. The licensing and add-on cost structure can also be difficult to predict as the business grows, since many features that feel essential are gated behind additional modules. These friction points are manageable given the overall value NetSuite provides, but they are real enough that they add to the total cost of ownership beyond just the subscription fee.
column10: NetSuite solves the core problem of having financial and operational data scattered across multiple disconnected systems. Before consolidating onto NetSuite, reconciling inventory levels with accounting records and generating accurate reports required pulling data from separate tools and doing a lot of manual work to make it all line up, which was both time consuming and prone to errors. NetSuite brought all of that into a single source of truth, meaning our inventory, accounts, and reporting are always in sync without any manual intervention. The biggest benefit has been the improvement in financial accuracy and the speed at which we can close our books and generate reports, which gives leadership better visibility into business performance and supports faster, more confident decision making. From an inventory standpoint, having real time visibility into stock levels integrated directly with our financials means we can manage purchasing and cash flow more strategically. Overall NetSuite has removed a significant amount of operational overhead and given our team the reliable data infrastructure needed to support growth across our ecommerce and wholesale channels. Powerful All-in-One Reporting and Inventory, but Complex and

column7: NetSuite offers a wide range of valuable features that have significantly improved our operations. Since transitioning to this software about five years ago, the experience has been highly positive. In my role within purchasing and inventory management, the system has greatly streamlined my workflow for example, having real-time access to inventory levels and purchasing data allows me to make faster, more accurate decisions without switching between multiple tools. Additionally, the centralized dashboard and automated processes reduce manual data entry and save me several hours each week, enabling me to focus on more strategic tasks.
column8: Certain processes, like generating reports or running searches, can be slower than ideal, particularly with large datasets. These challenges don’t outweigh the benefits, but they can impact efficiency in day-to-day use.
column10:
NetSuite solves several key challenges in purchasing and inventory management by centralizing data and automating critical processes. Instead of relying on multiple systems or manual tracking, it provides real-time visibility into inventory levels, purchase orders, and supplier information all in one place. This eliminates guesswork and reduces the risk of errors such as over-ordering or stockouts.

For me, this has made a significant impact on efficiency and accuracy. I can quickly assess what needs to be ordered, track incoming shipments, and manage inventory without switching between tools or spreadsheets. The automation of routine tasks like order creation and reporting also saves time and reduces manual data entry, allowing me to focus more on planning and decision-making rather than administrative work. column7: I like how flexible NetSuite is and how you can interact with it. It's getting easier and easier to connect it with other platforms, whether out of the box or paid services, or using the AI models. We have been able to connect different ways, like an Apple Shopify connection with third party Selena, Floj connecting our POS Square, and using ShipStation for generating labels, all done by CSV. That's the flexibility of those with some of the tools.
column8: Some of the modules seem like they were an afterthought or built in, which isn't great. The language and safe search features aren't always clear, and there are limitations in setup that just aren't obvious. This kind of hidden knowledge is frustrating. Knowing that NetSuite is primarily a general ledger would have been helpful and saved us time. The initial setup was really challenging because we didn't have any on-site IT experts, and the consultants weren't much help. We had to manually handle a lot of processes, which was tedious. Also, moving to a web store with a user-friendly interface was harder than we thought, so we ended up using Shopify instead of SuiteCommerce.

column7:
What I like best about NetSuite is that it provides a complete, cloud-based ERP solution that brings together finance, order management, inventory, and reporting in a single platform. This helps reduce dependency on multiple systems and improves overall operational efficiency.

NetSuite also offers strong customization capabilities through workflows, saved searches, and scripting, which makes it flexible to adapt to different business requirements. The integration capabilities with external platforms such as eCommerce, payment gateways, and third-party logistics providers are another major advantage.

Additionally, the real-time reporting and visibility across business processes help teams make faster and more informed decisions. The platform is scalable and suitable for growing businesses, making it a reliable long-term solution.
column8:
One of the challenges with NetSuite is that the user interface can sometimes feel less intuitive, especially for new users. Certain configurations and customizations may require technical expertise, which can increase dependency on developers or consultants.

Additionally, troubleshooting integrations and scripts can sometimes be time-consuming, and documentation for specific use cases is not always detailed. Performance can also occasionally slow down when working with large datasets or complex saved searches.
column10:
NetSuite helps streamline business operations by consolidating financial management, order processing, and inventory management into one unified platform. This reduces manual work, improves data accuracy, and enhances operational visibility.

It also simplifies integrations with external systems such as eCommerce platforms, payment providers, and logistics partners, allowing automation of key business processes. This has improved efficiency, reduced errors, and enabled faster delivery of solutions to clients.

Overall, NetSuite has helped improve productivity, reporting accuracy, and scalability across business operations. All-in-One, Scalable NetSuite ERP with Powerful Customization and

column7: I like NetSuite for its security because it allows us to comply directly with our company's security policies. The ease of use is a big plus as it greatly reduces the need for specialized training whenever someone new joins. I also appreciate that it's role-based, enabling the IT department to delegate access according to roles rather than individual users. It's great that I can work on any device and connection too. The fast timesheet reporting and the ability to enter timesheet data on one screen for projects, support cases, and tasks are really handy. Plus, the direct integration with the accounting systems in our company makes things seamless. Setting up NetSuite was very easy, though we did have a few user adoption challenges.
column8: I don't like that multi-currency settlement does not allow settlement between currencies in purchases or sales. Manufacturing planning also can't be customized depending on custom fields.
column10: I use NetSuite for fast timesheet reporting and easy data entry on one screen. It integrates directly with our accounting systems, enhancing security, ease of use, and role-based access to comply with policies and reduce specialized training needs. Efficient, Secure, but Needs Better

column7: The ability of NetSuite to offer end-to-end visibility across operations and finance in a single system is what I find most appealing. Strong process control is made possible by its integrated audit trails, approvals, and validations. With scripting, workflows, and saved searches, the platform is very customizable. It makes it possible to solve challenging business issues without requiring a lot of development work. It is an effective central hub because of its smooth integration with programs like Boomi. All things considered, it is a dependable and adaptable system for effectively managing financial operations.
column8: one disadvantage is that managing intricate logic (such as tolerance checks) frequently necessitates the use of scripting or saved searches as workarounds. Certain common restrictions, like line-level controls or partial billing of expenses, are not always clear-cut. Troubleshooting can be time-consuming due to unclear user interface and error messages. Some actions seem counterintuitive, such as managing system-generated records or excluding voided transactions. In general, obtaining advanced functionality may occasionally necessitate additional customization work rather than native support.
column10: By consolidating POs, vendor bills, and payments into a single system, NetSuite removes data silos and eliminates the need for cross-tool reconciliation. By preventing overbilling beyond tolerance limits, for example, it helps enforce financial control and lowers risk and errors. It saves you time on validations and approvals by streamlining the automation of manual processes (saved searches, workflows). It enhances reporting and visibility, enabling you to swiftly examine transaction details and discrepancies. It facilitates more seamless data synchronization between systems by integrating with programs like Boomi. NetSuite & it's

column7:
What I like most about NetSuite is that it brings everything together in one place. Rather than juggling separate tools for accounting, inventory, CRM, and reporting, it all runs within a single system. That makes day-to-day work smoother and saves a lot of time.

Another standout for me is the real-time visibility. I can quickly check financials, track orders, or review performance without having to wait for reports to be generated, which makes it easier to make faster, more informed decisions.

I also appreciate how customizable it is. As business needs change, you can adjust workflows, dashboards, and reports without having to switch to a completely different system. Overall, it feels like a scalable solution that can grow along with the business.
column8:
One thing I don’t like about NetSuite is that it can feel pretty complex, especially for new users. The interface isn’t always very intuitive, so it takes a while to get comfortable and really understand how everything works.

Another drawback is the cost. For smaller businesses, it can be quite expensive—not only the subscription, but also the implementation and customization. It’s simply not always the most budget-friendly option.

I’ve also found that some customizations and reports can be tricky to set up without technical help. At times you need external support or a specialist, which can slow progress and make changes take longer than expected.

Overall, even though it’s a powerful system, there’s a clear learning curve and some added costs that come with it.
column10:
One of the biggest problems NetSuite solves for me is reporting and overall data visibility. Before, pulling reports was time-consuming, and the information I needed was often scattered across different systems or buried in spreadsheets. Because of that, it was difficult to get a clear, real-time view of how the business was performing.

With NetSuite, reporting feels much more streamlined. I can generate real-time reports whenever I need them, without having to wait on someone else or manually compile data from multiple places. The dashboards are especially useful because they provide a quick snapshot of key metrics all in one place.

It also makes customization easier. I can tailor reports to specific requirements, which helps me get more meaningful insights rather than just generic data. This has improved decision-making, since I can rely on information that’s accurate and up to date.

Overall, it saves a lot of time, reduces manual effort, and makes reporting more reliable and accessible. NetSuite Brings Everything Together with Real-Time Visibility and

column10:
NetSuite is solving several operational challenges by providing an integrated platform for managing various business processes. 

Order Management – It streamlines order processing, ensuring accuracy and efficiency in managing customer and dealer orders.
Invoicing & Payments – Automates invoice generation and payment processing, reducing manual errors and improving financial accuracy.
Inventory & Listing Management – Helps track stock levels, manage product listings, and prevent pricing discrepancies across different sales channels.
Deduction & Chargeback Handling – Facilitates tracking and resolution of deductions, chargebacks, and compliance issues, minimizing financial losses.
Reporting & Analytics – Provides real-time insights into sales, inventory, and financials, enabling better decision-making.
Tax Compliance – Ensures proper tax calculations and compliance based on different state and regional requirements.
By centralizing these processes, NetSuite increases efficiency, reduces errors, and enhances overall operational visibility.

column7: NetSuite is powerful because it brings accounting, billing, purchasing, and financial reporting into one unified system, creating a single source of truth for the business. For finance teams, it helps automate key processes such as invoicing, revenue recognition, and approvals, which reduces manual work and helps cut down on errors. The platform is also highly scalable, allowing companies to grow from startup to enterprise without needing to switch ERPs along the way. NetSuite offers strong real-time reporting and dashboards as well, giving leadership clear visibility into overall financial performance. On top of that, its ability to integrate with tools like Salesforce and other operational systems makes it a solid backbone for a modern SaaS tech stack.
column8: One downside of NetSuite is that its interface can feel complex and not very intuitive for new users, so it often requires additional training to get people comfortable. Implementations and customizations can also be time-consuming and expensive, particularly when the system needs to be tailored to specific workflows. Some reporting and saved search functionality may require technical knowledge to build and maintain effectively. In addition, certain integrations with other systems can require middleware or extra development work. Finally, when you factor in licensing, implementation, and ongoing support, the overall cost can be high for smaller organizations.
column10: NetSuite addresses the challenge of financial data being scattered across multiple systems by bringing accounting, billing, purchasing, and reporting into a single platform. This creates one reliable source of truth for financial information and provides clearer visibility into the company’s overall performance. It also automates many manual tasks—such as invoicing, approvals, and revenue recognition—which saves time and helps reduce the risk of errors. As a result, the finance team can close the books more quickly and focus more on analysis instead of routine manual work. Overall, NetSuite supports more efficient scaling while maintaining strong financial controls and accurate reporting. Powerful, Scalable ERP with Strong Automation and

column7: I use NetSuite as my main ERP and financial/inventory system because it solves a lot of problems like inventory management, financials, payables, and receivables. Having all the business processes under one platform makes things easy to find, dependable for reporting, and robust to rely upon. What I like most about NetSuite is how customizable it is. It may not be great at any one thing, but you can tailor it to your needs. We were the first retailer of our kind on NetSuite, and we molded it to match our business needs. We've since grown and expanded upon our usage of the software far beyond what we originally intended. NetSuite allows us to modify the system whenever we have a specific business need, like controlling order capacity and delivery scheduling, by creating custom records that fit our requirements.
column8: The UI is slow, clunky, and outdated. I'm hopeful that NetSuite Next (next generation UI) fixes those issues. Navigating between transactions or records can be cumbersome. There also is a pretty steep learning curve around the backend language - understanding how fields and records are joined can be challenging at first. For starters, the look could feel more modern. The current UI feels like it's from the mid-2010s, and it sometimes performs like it. Waiting on fields to populate, sublists to load, transactions to save - it all just feels slow. Creating records, while similar across verticals, could be streamlined. It wasn't that easy. We got lucky in that we had a functional consultant live local to our office in northern IL, but even then we took a staged approach to implementation. Financials first, then AP and purchasing, then sales, AR, and inventory.
column9: NetSuite simplifies inventory management, financials, payables, and receivables by consolidating business processes on one platform. It's highly customizable, making it dependable for reporting and tailoring to specific business needs. Customizable and Reliable but
column10: Do your due diligence.

column7: What I like best about NetSuite is its flexibility and how much we can tailor it to real business processes. Saved searches, custom records, workflows, Suitelets, and scripts make it possible to build solutions that fit our team instead of forcing everything into a rigid standard process. It has been especially helpful for improving reporting, automating repetitive tasks, and connecting inventory, sales, and finance data in one place. For a growing business, that level of customization and visibility adds a lot of value.
column8: My main frustration with NetSuite is that while it is highly flexible, it often takes significant setup, customization, and technical knowledge to make processes work efficiently. Some native reports and search limitations mean teams still need workarounds or custom development to get exactly what they need. The UI in certain areas also feels dated, and performance can slow down when using saved searches, scripts, or large datasets. Overall, it is powerful, but not always as user-friendly or efficient out of the box as it could be.
column10: Before NetSuite, a lot of our tracking and reporting had to be done manually across spreadsheets and separate processes. column7: NetSuite’s biggest strength is that it serves as a true single source of truth across finance, operations, projects, and reporting. Having transactions, job costing, inventory, purchasing, and financials all tied together in one system dramatically improves visibility and accountability. The flexibility of saved searches, workflows, and custom fields allows us to adapt the system to our business without heavy custom development, and the real-time access to data supports better decision-making. From a scalability standpoint, NetSuite also gives confidence that the platform can grow with the business rather than needing to be replaced.
column8: While NetSuite is powerful, it can feel overly complex for everyday users, especially when simple tasks require deep configuration or workarounds. Some reporting and UI behaviors (such as recalculation delays, limited control over certain system fields, or inconsistent filter behavior between reports and saved searches) can be frustrating. There is also a noticeable learning curve, and changes that seem small on the surface often require admin involvement, scripts, or workflows. Finally, performance and transparency around background processes (like project profitability recalculations) could be improved to help users better trust and understand what they’re seeing.
column10: NetSuite is solving the challenge of fragmented systems and disconnected data by bringing financials, purchasing, inventory, projects, time, and reporting into one integrated platform. This reduces manual reconciliation, duplicate data entry, and errors that occur when information lives in multiple tools. As a result, we have better visibility into job costs, project profitability, and operational performance, which helps us make more informed decisions. The system also enforces consistent processes and controls as the business grows, allowing us to scale operations without losing financial accuracy or oversight. A True Single Source of Truth with Flexible Customization and

column7: NetSuite allows our company one central, streamlined ERP system for our employees to utilize. Where NetSuite has its limits, it integrates well with third party companies. We use it with Blend ERP and RF Smart. The consultants are very knowledgeable and eager to help build out what we need for customizations. They do their best to work within the original SOW and use native NetSuite solutions to workflows before talking custom solutions. Additional costs for custom scripts have been reasonable. I found the implementation process invaluable; the consulting team walks you through every piece and provides instruction on how to configure and customize the system yourself. MyLearn also has videos on how to do pretty much everything. I started as a beginner and now I'm a more advanced user; I use admin role and edit our advanced PDFs, customize transaction forms, and manage list data with csv imports on a regular basis. The thing that surprised me the most is the translation functions. Any language is available to the user. That includes system use and most of the MyLearn videos. Overall, I would recommend NetSuite as a solid ERP solution for mid-sized to larger companies.

column7: The saved search and Workbook analytics framework is the standout feature — once you invest time learning it, you can build surprisingly powerful custom views. For example, I built Open WO and Open PO workbooks with formula fields for Qty Remaining, Days Until Due, and aging buckets that give my team real-time prioritization visibility we didn't have before. That flexibility is genuinely valuable. The fact that MRP, inventory, financials, and work orders live in one system also eliminates a class of integration problems — no syncing between a standalone MRP tool and a separate ERP. For a startup building out supply chain from scratch, having one system of record is a meaningful foundation even if execution against it requires heavy lifting.
column8: The manufacturing and MRP modules feel underbuilt for anything beyond simple discrete BOMs. We ran into a BOM direction error that caused fractional quantity issues in wafer singulation work orders — a basic lot sizing problem that required manual intervention to diagnose and fix. Lot and serial traceability doesn't work cleanly out of the box for multi-tier supply chains; we had to build workarounds using the Inventory Numbers saved search instead of a purpose-built genealogy view. The UI is dated and navigation is non-intuitive — finding the right saved search or transaction type involves too many clicks. Onboarding and support are heavily consultant-dependent, and documentation for advanced features like SuiteQL or Workbook formula syntax is sparse. No meaningful AI or intelligence layer exists in the modules we use — no demand sensing, no anomaly detection, nothing that reduces planner cognitive load.
column10: Centralizing MRP, work order management, and material traceability across a multi-tier semiconductor packaging supply chain — spanning foundry to assembly to optics. The single system of record for open POs and WOs is the core value: before NetSuite, visibility required pulling data from multiple sources and reconciling manually. Now a planner can see WO status, material availability, and aging in one place. The unexpected benefit has been using saved searches as a lightweight BI layer — with enough formula field work, you can approximate dashboards that would otherwise need a separate analytics tool. ROI is real but heavily dependent on internal investment in configuration; the product doesn't deliver that value on its own. Powerful Analytics, but MRP and Traceability Need Major

column7:
NetSuite is a robust and scalable ERP platform that is used frequently for daily finance and accounting operations such as invoicing, reconciliations, reporting, and compliance. Its wide range of features, real-time data visibility, and strong system controls help improve efficiency, accuracy, and decision-making across the organization. Once users become familiar with the interface, the system is easy to navigate and becomes a core operational tool.

The platform integrates well with other applications and offers flexibility during implementation to match business requirements. Customer support resources, documentation, and partner assistance are generally helpful in resolving issues and supporting system usage. Being cloud-based, NetSuite allows easy access and consistent usage across teams, making it reliable for regular and long-term business needs.
column8:
There are some areas where improvement is required. Due to the depth of features, new users may face a learning curve, which can affect ease of use during the initial phase. Implementation timelines may extend when advanced customizations or multiple integrations are involved, and simplifying setup and navigation would improve user adoption.

Customer support response times can sometimes vary based on issue priority and support plans, and faster resolution would enhance the overall experience. Additionally, system performance optimization and more intuitive configuration options would help ensure smoother daily usage and better utilization of features.
column10: NetSuite solves the problem of managing multiple finance and accounting processes across different systems by bringing everything onto a single, integrated platform. It improves data accuracy, real-time visibility, and control over financial operations such as reporting, reconciliations, compliance, and audits. By automating workflows and centralizing information, NetSuite reduces manual effort, minimizes errors, and helps in faster and more informed decision-making. Comprehensive Cloud ERP That Streamlines Finance and

column7:
NetSuite is strong for managing warehouse operations once it’s properly configured. 
Inventory tracking, stock visibility, and order processing are all handled in one system, which reduces errors and manual work. 
It works well when dealing with higher volumes and multiple workflows across procurement, fulfilment, and reporting. 
For established warehouses, it provides structure and control that spreadsheets can’t.
column8:
The system has a steep learning curve, especially for warehouse staff who just need to move stock quickly. 
The interface can feel slow and overly complex for simple day-to-day tasks like picking, receiving, or adjustments. 
Changes and customisations often require admin knowledge or external consultants, which adds time and cost.
 Training is essential, otherwise productivity can drop.
column10:
NetSuite helps centralise warehouse operations by keeping inventory, orders, and reporting in one place. 
It reduces stock discrepancies, improves traceability, and supports better planning across inbound and outbound operations. 
While it takes time to implement properly, it scales well for growing warehouses and complex logistics environments. 
The main benefit is having accurate, real-time operational data instead of relying on manual tracking. Powerful Warehouse Control, But a Steep Learning Curve and Complex

column8: At times, I find that Netsuite support is not particularly helpful, especially when it comes to major functionalities that should work but don't. column10:
Primarily, Netsuite serves as a financial hub, and for most businesses we implement, financial management is the top priority. It makes sense that Netsuite handles our accounting and financials above all else. In addition, there are numerous integration options. With a wide range of third-party integration middlewares, including Netsuite's own FarApp and trusted partners like Celigo and Boomi, there are plenty of choices available. If you have an internal team or prefer to avoid ongoing integration costs, Netsuite SuiteScripts also provide an effective way to manage integrations.

column10:
NetSuite is an Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system that solves a handful of major problems for growing and mid-sized businesses by forcing structure and centralization.

column7: NetSuite is a powerful and comprehensive ERP system that provides excellent visibility across finance, operations, and reporting. Its automation capabilities and integration options add real value, especially for growing companies. However, the user interface can feel complex at times, and customization often requires technical support, which can slow down implementation. Overall, it’s a strong solution I’d recommend for organizations seeking scalability and robust financial control.
column8: The reporting tools, although flexible, aren’t always user-friendly for quick analysis, and small changes often need admin intervention. Additionally, system performance can slow down during heavy processing periods, and interface updates lag behind more modern SaaS tools.
column10:
NetSuite helps solve several key challenges in our organization:

Centralized Financial Management: It consolidates accounting, payments, and receivables in one system, reducing manual reconciliation and errors.

Real-Time Visibility: Provides up-to-date dashboards and reports, allowing us to monitor cash flow, liquidity, and loan activity in real time.

Process Automation: Automates routine workflows like invoicing, approvals, and bank reconciliations, saving time and reducing operational risk.

Scalability & Compliance: Supports our growth while ensuring adherence to internal policies and regulatory requirements, including audit readiness.

Benefits: These capabilities improve efficiency, enhance decision-making, and provide confidence that our financial operations are accurate, compliant, and transparent. Powerful ERP Solution with Excellent Automation and

column7: <v ->My name is Paul, I'm a president of the IT consulting</v> industry and I give NetSuite ERP-ing nine out of 10. We use NetSuite ERP for all our purchasing, sales and financial reporting. What I like best about NetSuite ERP is it does the complete business cycle from purchase to payment, from lead to order, order to cash and records your report and it's all web based. So it's anytime, anywhere access. What I dislike about NetSuite ERP is it's constantly adding new features, which I have to learn and understand more about to see if I want to use them or not. column7: I’ve been a NetSuite user for 8 years, and it continues to impress me with how seamlessly it brings accounting, operations, and reporting together in one centralized system. The ability to customize dashboards, automate workflows, and create detailed saved searches has made our financial processes much more efficient and transparent. NetSuite’s scalability has allowed our business to grow without outgrowing the platform, and the recent updates have only improved usability and performance. I also appreciate the continuous improvements Oracle makes to enhance reporting, intercompany transactions, and automation tools.
column8: While NetSuite is a powerful platform, the learning curve can be steep for new users due to the sheer depth of functionality. Some of the interface elements feel dated, and certain customizations still require technical support or scripting assistance. Additionally, support response times can vary depending on the issue severity. However, once the system is properly configured, the benefits far outweigh these challenges.
column10: NetSuite has helped us centralize and streamline our entire accounting and operations process — from order management and inventory control to intercompany transactions and consolidated financial reporting. Before NetSuite, many of these workflows were handled manually or across multiple systems, leading to inefficiencies and errors. Now, everything flows through one connected platform, giving us real-time visibility into performance and reducing month-end close time significantly. Features like automated eliminations, cross-charge workbench, and advanced intercompany journal entries have eliminated redundant work and improved accuracy across entities. Overall, NetSuite has allowed our team to work smarter, faster, and with much more confidence in our data. Powerful, Scalable, and Efficient—But Expect a

column7: The program is logically organized with a clear, yet customizable, path through the general sales funnel. I love that NetSuite allows for fully custom records that address specific and sometimes niche requirements of one's business. With workflows, one does not need to be a developer to be able to create useful, time-saving automations. If you are lucky enough to be paired with a truly knowledgeable partner, the implementation can be highly effective. It is important to be sure to ask a lot of questions and confirm that one's implementation partner really understands the business needs. We love the additional professional support we receive from our Advanced Customer Support team. If there are projects that we just cannot seem to handle on our own, we know that we will receive excellent support from our ACS team. Our specific instance has many integrations. The ability to integrate with NetSuite means that our satellite locations can retain some autonomy with which supplemental programs they use but have all details reflected in NetSuite for the enterprise at large to access.
column8: There are limitations in saved searches that do cause a good deal of frustration at times. Although, NetSuite has developed a more robust way to perform the same type of searches but in a farther-reaching way in the analytics workbooks. Some of the connections can be difficult to sus out when creating reports and searches. we have the a perspective of working with NetSuite as both a partner and a customer. We recently implemented NetSuite Financials First and NetSuite Projects internally, and the results have been transformative.

Fromn a financial management standpoint, NetSuite has given us a centralized, real-time view of our financial health. Processes that used to be manual and time-consuming, like month-end close, project expense tracking, and revenue recognition, are now streamlined and far less error-prone. The automation features and built-in compliance checks have saved us significant hours each month.

On the project side, NetSuite Projects has brought much-needed clarity to our resource planning and project profitability tracking. Being able to connect financial data directly with project milestones and deliverables means our leadership team can make better decisions faster.

We don’t just recommend NetSuite to clients, we’ve adopted it ourselves because we believe in the product. In fact, we love it so much that we made the decision to purchase and fully integrate it into our own business operations. That speaks volumes about the confidence we have in the platform’s ability to deliver.
column8: Implementation requires careful planning to get the most out of the system. Its not a plug a play solution. There are wonderful partners in consulting firms that would be able to help implement something like this. Which will be another cost, but it's worth the investment if this is a long-term solution.
column10:
I will speak to the purposses of the boxes I checked off above. 

ERP Systems – Provided a single source of truth across financials, operations, and projects, replacing disconnected tools and spreadsheets.

Accounting – Automated month-end close, improved accuracy in reporting, and reduced manual reconciliation.

Discrete ERP – Allowed us to better manage and track project-specific deliverables with direct links to costs and revenue.

CRM – Centralized our customer data, improving visibility across sales and service teams.

Billing – Standardized invoicing and automated recurring billing, reducing delays and errors.

Core HR – Streamlined employee records, time tracking, and approvals into one system.

Project-Based ERP – Connected financial performance directly to project milestones, enabling faster, more informed decision-making.

Distribution ERP – Improved visibility into inventory, order management, and fulfillment timelines.

These capabilities have not only saved us time and reduced operational risk, but they’ve also improved cross-departmental collaboration. The biggest benefit is being able to see accurate, real-time data across the business, which lets our leadership respond quickly to changes and opportunities. NetSuite Financials and Projects – Game Changer for

column7:
how easy it makes it to manage and understand our financials in real time. The platform gives us quick access to key reports like the Income Statement, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow, and allows us to trace data back to its source with just a few clicks. It also supports great collaboration — multiple team members can work on the same data set with different levels of access, all without version conflicts.

Another standout is its wide range of integrations. Whether it's payroll software, payment platforms, or other systems, NetSuite connects easily with the tools we use daily, which has streamlined a lot of our processes. It's intuitive to use, and the learning curve is short, which makes onboarding new team members much easier.
column8:
One thing I dislike about NetSuite is that some actions can be a bit slow to load. For example, opening certain records or navigating between pages can take longer than expected. It’s not a major issue, but it does affect the user experience during busy periods.

Additionally, the user interface feels a bit outdated and visually flat — mostly shades of gray — which can make the experience feel less dynamic. It would be great to have more customization options for the layout or theme to make it feel more modern and engaging.
column10:
NetSuite is helping us centralize and streamline our financial operations, which has improved visibility, accuracy, and collaboration across teams.

Before NetSuite, it was harder to track where numbers came from or to ensure everyone was working off the same data. Now, with real-time reporting and audit trails, we can easily trace transactions, monitor cash flow, and generate financial statements quickly and reliably.

Its integration capabilities have also reduced manual work — connecting payroll, payments, and other systems has saved us time and minimized errors.

Overall, NetSuite gives us a scalable, reliable system that supports better decision-making and more efficient processes.

column7: I find NetSuite incredibly beneficial for my role as a senior bookkeeper. Its consolidation capabilities are invaluable, allowing me to view all entities on the same screen, which vastly improves efficiency by eliminating the tedious task of downloading separate Excel files for comparison. The ability to generate consolidated reports on screen and make live adjustments is a significant time-saver. I also appreciate the convenience of features like the option to edit transactions directly and the ease of accessing well-detailed reports, which enhances my day-to-day workflow. Moreover, NetSuite's integration with third-party software, such as Ramp, ensures a seamless experience with minimal manual work, which is critical when managing multiple platforms. This integration capability is especially advantageous for a large company dealing with various softwares like Rippling and ensures that all software components work together smoothly, including bank feeds for all banks. Overall, NetSuite’s comprehensive features and strong integration capabilities provide a robust platform that supports my accounting needs efficiently.

column7:
Order Creation Workflow:

We initiate the order creation process in Salesforce, and Oracle NetSuite seamlessly handles the actual order generation, ensuring efficiency and reliability.
The workflow between Salesforce and NetSuite has been smooth, minimizing complexities and delays during order processing.
Contract Management:

All contract details are created and stored in NetSuite, providing a centralized and robust system for managing critical business agreements.
Integration Capabilities:

NetSuite offers excellent integration with Salesforce, enabling contract details to be sent back to Salesforce effortlessly.
This integration enhances collaboration between systems, ensuring data consistency and a streamlined workflow for both sales and contract management teams.
Overall, Oracle NetSuite simplifies major operational tasks, such as order creation and contract management, while providing strong integration capabilities that bridge the gap between Salesforce and NetSuite to ensure smooth business processes.

column7:
We've been using Oracle NetSuite for a few years now, and it has become an essential part of our operations. As a growing company with complex financial and operational needs, we needed a system that could scale with us, and NetSuite has delivered.

Key strengths we've experienced:

Robust Financial Management: NetSuite’s general ledger, budgeting, and reporting tools are powerful. We’ve been able to streamline our month-end close, generate real-time financial reports, and improve overall accuracy across the board.

Customizable Dashboards & Reports: The real-time dashboards and customizable reporting features are excellent. They give our leadership team clear, actionable insights into cash flow, sales performance, and KPIs at a glance.

Cloud-Based Efficiency: Being cloud-based, we’ve benefited from remote access, seamless updates, and strong data security without the burden of maintaining local servers.

Integration & Scalability: NetSuite integrates well with our other systems and has helped unify data across departments. It also offers the flexibility to support future growth and more advanced modules if we choose to expand.

Overall:
NetSuite has helped us gain better visibility, improve financial controls, and make smarter decisions. It’s a solid investment for any business looking for an all-in-one ERP solution that’s flexible and scalable.
column8: One major drawback of NetSuite is the cost of support and optimization. While the platform has a lot of functionality, any time we want assistance implementing a feature we’re not currently using—or even just want guidance on how to better utilize existing tools—it almost always comes with an additional fee. This makes it difficult to fully leverage the system without increasing our ongoing spend and can be frustrating for teams that want to improve efficiency but are limited by the cost of accessing expert help.
column10:
NetSuite is helping us centralize and streamline our financial operations, which has been a major improvement from our previous disconnected systems. It gives us real-time visibility into our financials, helps us close the books faster, and improves accuracy across our reporting.

We’re also able to manage purchasing, inventory, and order processing all within one platform, which cuts down on manual work, reduces errors, and improves collaboration between departments.

column8: I find the complexity of NetSuite both its best and worst aspect. Initially, when acquiring a NetSuite instance, the expectation might be that it will function seamlessly, but that's not the case. The process is akin to receiving just a foundation of a house, with the actual build-out taking time. Typically, there's about a three-month window before going live, which involves constructing everything according to the specific industry and vertical needs, and this can be very intricate. If there were a way to simplify this process, particularly during implementation, it would greatly relieve some pain points. Often, organizations encounter issues from the onset because data is input incorrectly or lacks proper structure, which isn't a flaw with NetSuite itself but more to do with how businesses rush to get it operational, likely due to its high price point and the complexity of the sales cycle. Simplifying implementation would prevent many issues, ensuring data is correctly placed and clean from the start, ultimately solving numerous problems down the road.

column8: User acceptance has been challenging, and overall user adoption remains low. It's difficult to convince my end users to embrace Netsuite, especially those who aren't required to use it, largely because the user interface leaves much to be desired. Even with the introduction of the new Redwood design, the experience hasn't improved significantly. Another issue I have is that NetSuite isn't as open source as some other platforms, which limits development opportunities. This reliance on their internal developers means clients are often left waiting for enhancement requests to be addressed. I also wish Netsuite would allow backend scripting, particularly for features like bank feeds, which are currently very frustrating to use. I also don't like that it is so incredibly expensive. column7: NetSuite is a powerful and robust platform that brings together many facets of our business. I really appreciate having a single source of truth for everything from project management to financial reporting. The ability to tie so many operational and financial processes together in one system is incredibly valuable. I also like knowing there's much more we could unlock — it's a tool with a lot of depth, and I wish we had the time and resources to take fuller advantage of all it offers. Integration with other modules has gone smoothly so far. It is a tool we use daily!
column8:
Despite its strengths, there are areas where NetSuite feels disjointed or underdeveloped. For example:

Some fields are only usable in resource management and not available elsewhere, which creates unnecessary limitations.

Project task functionality feels like an afterthought, not a fully integrated part of the system.

It's frustrating that Service Items can’t be linked to employees without a custom script — especially when selling professional services is the majority of our business.

We've had a support ticket open for over a year regarding a syntax error, and there’s still no resolution.

We also have an open support ticket related to mass updates on effective-dated fields in employee records. Every year, when we perform these updates, the system wipes out the “special skills” section — even when using forms where that section is hidden. Despite multiple attempts and troubleshooting, the issue remains unresolved after more than a year.

Nothing here would keep me from recommending the system to others. In fact, some of these issues and those not mentioned convinced me to work towards certification.
column10:
NetSuite gives us a centralized system to manage our operations, financials, and project-based activities all in one place. It replaces disconnected tools and spreadsheets by integrating project management, time tracking, billing, reporting, and general ledger into a single platform. This helps ensure consistency, improves visibility across departments, and supports better decision-making.

It also gives our teams access to real-time data, which is critical for both day-to-day operations and long-term planning. For a business like ours — with a mix of R&D, professional services, and manufacturing — having one system to manage everything reduces manual effort, minimizes errors, and provides the foundation for scalability. Powerful Platform, Comprehensive but

column7: What I like best about NetSuite is honestly how everything’s in one place — from AR and AP to full financial reporting. I use it daily, and it's super intuitive once you get the hang of it. The implementation was smooth, and it integrates really well with other software we use, which makes processes way more efficient. Also, shoutout to their customer support — they’re quick, helpful, and actually know what they’re doing. It just makes life a lot easier when your system works with you, not against you.
column8: What I dislike about NetSuite is that some customizations can get a bit tricky and usually need a specialist or consultant to help out — it’s not always plug-and-play when it comes to more complex setups. The interface, while functional, can feel a bit outdated in parts, and certain tasks take a few more clicks than they should.
column10: NetSuite has made bank matching and cash reconciliation so much smoother. Before, it was a very manual and time-consuming process, but now with the matching rules, it’s way quicker to reconcile transactions. It flags mismatches easily, and we can sort out issues without digging through spreadsheets. This not only saves time but also gives a much clearer and more accurate picture of our cash position on a daily basis, which is a huge help for cash flow management and reporting.

column7: NetSuite provides a single source of truth across finance, operations, inventory, CRM, and reporting. The system is highly customizable, and saved searches/dashboards make it easy to surface real-time insights. The automation it brings to financial processes—billing, revenue recognition, purchasing, approvals—has significantly reduced manual work. Its flexibility and scalability make it a great long-term platform as a business grows.
column8: The initial implementation takes time and expertise, and configuration can be complex if you don’t have experienced support. Some modules and customizations come at an additional cost. However, once properly set up, the system is extremely reliable and efficient
column10: NetSuite breaks down data silos by bringing all departments together on a single platform. With this integration, the finance team enjoys real-time visibility, making reconciliation easier and reducing the time needed for month-end close. Operations have improved inventory tracking and more efficient order management. Leadership benefits from precise reporting and forecasting, no longer needing to depend on spreadsheets. Overall, NetSuite supports quicker decision-making and leads to more consistent business performance. Transformative Platform for Scalable, Automated

column9: We've solved many problems and offered many features that are either fundamental to our business or help us not to need multiple, disparate systems. There is a huge value in having so many solutions in one system for starters (Accounting, Sales, Professional Services, R&D, Support) that most people overlook as they hunt for the next hip, shiny tool. Keeping data tight, offering easy visibility for every department into the health of customers, budgets, and product lines helps the business move more fluidly and save money that would be wasted adding on other systems (especially integrations), training users, maintaining the system, and potentially hiring another administrator.
column10: Spend a lot of time in design phase, getting Chart of Accounts in order. Spend nearly as much time with UAT and general adoption. Beyond that, the best thing I can recommend is to find yourself a full time NetSuite admin who will OWN the system, defending it from bad data and bandaid solutions; if your admin is good, the salary you pay will pay back quickly with the features that can be built in and the time-saving (and ultimately head count-saving) that role can bring.

column8:
The integration partners do not always pull through basic, native NetSuite functionality which means that if you are going to use one of those add-on providers, such as AP workflows, then you may have to add additional procedures that feel redundant based on what NetSuite is capable of support - for example, Prepaid Expenses, Amortization Schedules can be setup on the Vendor Bill line item at time of the Bill creation in NetSuite, but I haven't found an add-on AP workflow tool yet that is able to support that functionality if the Bill starts in their system and pushes to NetSuite. This forces Accounting to manage another step after sync to either modify the bill coding and line item to add the amortization schedule or have an entirely separate process to establish amortization schedules that will not be attached to the related and originating Prepaid Bill.

I think native features must be supported for them to be a recommended add-on provider.

column10:
Financial Fragmentation – Hospitals often use multiple systems for billing, payroll, purchasing, and reporting. NetSuite centralizes everything, reducing duplicate data entry and errors.

Supply Chain Inefficiencies – From medical supplies to equipment, NetSuite improves tracking, automates reordering, and reduces stockouts or overstock.

Regulatory & Compliance Complexity – Built-in audit trails and reporting tools help maintain compliance with healthcare regulations and financial standards.

Lack of Real-Time Visibility – Administrators get dashboards that show financial health, patient billing trends, vendor performance, and operational KPIs in real time.

Manual Processes – Automates approvals, vendor payments, and contract management, freeing staff to focus on patient care and strategic initiatives.

column7: What I like best about NetSuite is how everything’s in one place. It’s super helpful having finance, inventory, and CRM all connected—it saves a ton of time jumping between systems. I also really appreciate how customizable it is. We’ve been able to tweak dashboards and workflows to fit exactly how our team operates, which makes day-to-day tasks smoother. Once you get the hang of it, it really helps keep things organized and moving efficiently.
column8: The steep learning curve in NetSuite is what I find most annoying. Although it's powerful, it can take some time to learn how to use some of the functions, especially the more sophisticated ones. Some procedures seem too complex, and in order to get everything set up correctly, you frequently need technical assistance or a consultant. Additionally, the price can go up rapidly, particularly if you require third-party integrations or customizations. Once it's dialed in, it functions well, but getting there isn't always easy.

column7: I have been working with NetSuite for over 11 years. I enjoy that they seem to be updating the product and features continuously. Sometimes this creates a few hiccups, but overall they seem very prepared to take their customers with them as they modernize. I enjoy the ability to control so many aspects of my accounting system and the depths of their reporting capacity. The representatives we've worked with over the years have always been incredibly responsive so I believe you get what you pay for with this product. We use it every day and across office locations with ease. I was not there when we implemented NetSuite, but from what I know it was very easy. We have integrated several features on the years and it was always fairly seamless.
column8: As I mentioned about updates, sometimes it can cause minor disruptions and it can happen quickly. When they deprecated 1099s that was a little stressful because we found out right before year end. However, in the end it was worth it because the program we got was even better. Just painful in the transition period. Also, there are some areas where I think the reporting and processes were built by IT professionals and not accountants. I'm sure they're tired of hearing that, since us accountants can appear to be giant complainers. The reporting is so complex that sometimes it's hard to know what format to start with when you want to do something. Also, we ran into an issue where two features combined do not give us the result promised (income statement with all active accounts, including zero balances but not inactive accounts). We have been through their service desk and no one seems to understand why it doesn't produce that right information and keeps including inactive accounts in the final report. Lastly, support is incredibly helpful but be prepared to deal with time zone issues and potentially timing issues.

column7:
NetSuite's multi-currency functionality is a massive strength for us. It has fully automated our currency conversions and revaluations, which used to be a major source of stress. The ability to track transactions in multiple currencies simultaneously, with automatic updates from trusted financial sources, ensures our data is always accurate and compliant. We now have a single, real-time view of our global financial position. The dashboards give our leadership team instant access to key performance indicators (KPIs) like foreign exchange gains and losses, cash flow by currency, and more. 

Integration was made a breeze thanks for NetSuite's premium support, the Advanced Customer Support team, and our 3rd party NetSuite implementation partners. It's a system we use daily, and continue to learn new features and innovations.
column8: While the long-term benefits are clear, there was a significant learning curve during the initial transition. The sheer depth of the platform means that it takes time for a team accustomed to a simpler system like QuickBooks to fully grasp all the functionalities and configurations. The initial setup and customization, while necessary for our specific needs, was a complex and resource-intensive process.
column10: Before NetSuite, we were likely dealing with manual data entry, complex spreadsheet formulas, and a lack of real-time exchange rate updates. This is a massive headache for a currency exchange bank, as it's prone to human error, delays, and can lead to financial losses. Our previous system, like many basic accounting programs, probably provided only a fragmented view of your financial health. Getting a consolidated, real-time snapshot of your cash flow, profitability, and foreign exchange gains/losses across different currencies was likely a manual, end-of-period task. Using spreadsheets for budgeting is a common challenge. It's time-consuming, difficult to collaborate on, and lacks the ability to easily model "what-if" scenarios. In a currency exchange business where rates can fluctuate wildly, a static budget is practically useless. A smaller system like QuickBooks can't handle the increasing complexity of a growing financial institution. As you add more customers, transactions, and currencies, the old system becomes a bottleneck, hindering growth. Amazing upgrade coming from

column7: This software is excellent; it integrates all processes in a structured way. You cannot proceed to step 2 without completing step 1, which helps ensure that everything is done in order. Additionally, you can automate many tasks, and the interface is user-friendly. Most transactions can be customized to fit the needs of your organization. Everything is interconnected, allowing you to trace all related records from any transaction.
column8: The implementation process was a complete disaster. We have been using this software for almost three years, and we are still fixing issues from the initial implementation. There have been constant changes within the organization, and we have had four different account managers. Support is difficult to understand, and they work in a different time zone, resulting in inconvenient calls at 7 or 8 PM. The renewal process was terrible; it took months to negotiate, and the pricing was high. The mobile app is not very user-friendly, and no one has clearly communicated all the features we can use with the software. Definetely, you need to hire an consultant additional to netsuite for a smooth integration.

column8:
Most times NetSuite does not sync bank transactions as required, and this causes delays in our reconciliation process, we have to manually import the bank statement transactions to complete reconciliation. The system has its flaws sometimes, some roles can not do certain tasks or edits which are relevant to the role.
One can not edit a journal entry from the controller and Adminstrator roles.
column10:
NetSuite is enabling easy recording and access of financial information for both products and services.

column7:
Overall, NetSuite has been a powerful tool for managing our business operations, especially when it comes to handling multiple subsidiaries. The ability to consolidate data and run reports across different entities has been extremely helpful. I also really appreciate the analysis and reporting functions—being able to build saved searches and customize reports makes it much easier to track key metrics and make informed decisions.
We've been using NetSuite daily, and overall, it's been a solid platform for our business. One of the features I appreciate most is the ability to manage multiple subsidiaries. The reporting and analysis tools are also excellent—c
Ease of Implementation: Implementation was smooth. The system was easy to adopt thanks to the available guides, videos, and resources in SuiteAnswers.

Ease of Integration: Integrating NetSuite with other tools was also straightforward, especially with the help of SuiteAnswers and clear documentation.

Customer Support: Unfortunately, our experience with the previous ACS (Advanced Customer Support) team was not great. We didn’t receive the level of support we expected, which made troubleshooting certain issues more difficult than it should have been.
column8: Budgeting Module: One of the areas where NetSuite falls short is budgeting. It's not as intuitive or flexible as other parts of the system, and generating clear reports or making adjustments is more difficult than expected.
column10:
As a plumbing company with multiple branches and subsidiaries, NetSuite has helped us centralize our operations. We’re able to manage everything under one system with a single login, which simplifies our workflow and saves time.

Instead of juggling multiple systems or accounts for each location, we can now view financials, track performance, and run consolidated reports across all entities. This has improved our visibility, accuracy, and decision-making, while also streamlining day-to-day accounting and reporting tasks.

Additionally, its flexibility in building and customizing cases allows teams to tailor processes to meet specific business needs without compromising efficiency or control.
column8: One of the key drawbacks we've experienced is the system's slowness, particularly noticeable toward the end of the day. This lag can hinder productivity during critical periods, especially when teams are trying to wrap up tasks or meet end-of-day deadlines. While the platform offers robust capabilities, performance consistency remains an area with room for improvement. Optimizing load times and ensuring smoother navigation during peak usage hours would greatly enhance the overall user experience.
column10: One of NetSuite's standout features is the flexibility it offers within case management. Multiple teams across departments can engage with cases at any level of capacity, whether they're handling customer support, technical issues, or internal workflows. This cross-functional accessibility promotes collaboration and ensures that cases are handled efficiently from start to resolution. By allowing users to tailor case fields, workflows, and visibility settings, NetSuite supports a scalable and adaptable approach that aligns well with both small teams and enterprise-level operations. column8: Implementation and the customer service is a NIGHTMARE. Run away from this product as fast as you can. If you choose to purchase, make sure to document, record, and video everything in the sales process when you tell them what you want the system to do. The sales people just want to close a deal and make promises they can't keep, then pass you off to an implementation team that has no idea what was promised. You'll hear "that's not in scope" when you make a request to set up something the sales team said the software could do, and then Netsuite soaks you for more $$$$. They sell the contracts to an outside 3rd party, so there is no flexibility. Apologies are hollow and they take responsibility for nothing. We were delayed for a year while Netsuite had to do additional scripting. Payroll expired and we had to pay for that implementation twice due to Netsuite's delays. Then they didn't set up the third party administrator correctly for state taxes and we were charged penalties and interest for their failure to set it up, and now they want us to pay an additional $1000 to refile something that they didn't do correctly in the first place. If you buy Netsuite, I suggest you have a strong legal team.

column7:
We've been using NetSuite primarily for inventory management, and it's been a game changer for our operations. What stands out most is how seamlessly it tracks inventory across multiple locations, giving us real-time visibility into stock levels, reorder points across our omni-channel business.

NetSuite's centralized dashboard makes it easy to monitor KPIs and inventory performance, and the customizable reporting tools give us the insights we need to make smarter business decisions.

Another key benefit is the integration with other business processes like order management, RFID API, and accounting. Everything is streamlined, which saves time and improves accuracy.

While there’s a bit of a learning curve initially, the support and training resources are strong, and once set up, NetSuite is incredibly powerful and flexible.

Overall, NetSuite has significantly improved our inventory control, efficiency, and visibility. Highly recommend it for any business looking for a robust, cloud-based inventory management solution
column8:
While NetSuite is a powerful tool, one area that can be frustrating—especially early on—is the learning curve. The system is highly customizable, which is great in the long run, but setting it up the way you need can be time-consuming and often requires outside consultants or significant internal resources.

Also, some of the user interface elements can feel a bit dated or unintuitive, especially when compared to more modern, lightweight platforms. It takes time for new users to feel comfortable navigating the system efficiently.
column10:
Before implementing NetSuite, we struggled with fragmented systems—inventory data was siloed across spreadsheets and different platforms, which led to delays, errors, and poor decision-making. NetSuite brought everything into one unified system, giving us real-time visibility into inventory levels across all locations, which is crucial for planning and fulfillment.

Another major issue it solved is manual and error-prone processes. With automated workflows for reordering, fulfillment, and reporting, we’ve significantly reduced human error and saved countless hours of manual work. This allows our team to focus more on strategy rather than just keeping up with daily operations.

NetSuite has also helped us scale—as our business grows, we’re able to handle more SKUs, more transactions, and more complexity without needing to overhaul our systems.

Overall, it’s improved our accuracy, speed, and confidence in inventory-related decisions, which directly impacts customer satisfaction and our bottom line. NetSuite Review – Excellent Inventory

column7: NetSuite’s CRM is tailor-made for the complexities of E-commerce. From the moment a customer lands on our site to post-purchase follow-up, NetSuite provides a 360-degree view of the customer journey. This has allowed us to personalize experiences, improve retention, and drive repeat purchases like never before. One of the biggest game-changers has been how well NetSuite integrates with our inventory management, order processing, and fulfillment systems. We no longer have to juggle multiple platforms or worry about data syncing issues. Everything is connected, accurate, and updated in real time.
column8: NetSuite is a premium solution, and its pricing can be hard to manage for small businesses. Costs can escalate quickly with add-ons, customizations, and user licenses. While NetSuite is highly customizable, making those customizations often requires technical expertise or third-party consultants. Simple changes can sometimes feel unnecessarily complex. The reporting tools can be difficult to master. Creating custom reports often requires a deep understanding of NetSuite and in person learning labs aren't offered very often.
column10: NetSuite integrates directly with our EDI provider, allowing us to automatically exchange documents like purchase orders, invoices, shipping notices, and inventory updates with our customers and suppliers. This means no more manual data entry or switching between systems—everything flows through one unified platform. With NetSuite, EDI transactions are fully automated. Orders come in, inventory is updated, and shipping details are sent out—all without human intervention. This has drastically reduced errors and freed up our team to focus on higher-value tasks. We’ve set up custom workflows and alerts in NetSuite to notify us of any EDI issues—like failed transmissions or missing data—so we can resolve them quickly. This proactive approach has improved our reliability and strengthened our partner relationships. As we’ve added more trading partners and increased our transaction volume, NetSuite has scaled with us effortlessly. Whether we’re processing dozens or thousands of EDI documents a day, the system remains fast, stable, and easy to manage. NetSuite has made our EDI operations faster, more accurate, and far more efficient. It’s helped us meet partner requirements, reduce costs, and deliver a better experience to our customers—all while giving us the tools to grow confidently. Built for

column7:
NetSuite is great at keeping business information and workflows organized for non-technical users.
Not to mention, the level of flexibility available to us to customize the interface, integrate APIs, and interact with 3rd party applications allows us to keep over 80% of our business localized to NetSuite. 
Which is incredible! As, a company we rarely have to leave the NetSuite site to do most of our work.
column8:
However, while NetSuite is incredibly easy to use and integrate niche business requirements with, it's not always a straightforward path to get there.
When we start a new development project we are sometimes left ping-ponging between wanting to keep the project within the NetSuite environment and building a new website to have more control.
 "How can we keep this on NetSuite instead of building a new website", is the question we ask 9 times out of 10.
NetSuite does not support access to things like the browser's DOM, nor can we add custom HTML/CSS/JS to anything beyond an inline HTML field. - There was a time where I built a whole form, complete with tables, image uploads, radio buttons, text fields, etc. ALL within a singular InlineHTML form field. This is not a NetSuite supported interaction, and required quite a few workaround "hacks" with Ajax and jQuery to be able to gather information from the html field and send it back to NetSuite to record the data in a record.
Another project, we needed to build a NetSuite "assistant"-like page that would pull data from different records and then display cabinets that met the criteria selected. This was a primarily front end facing project to show to customers. Naturally, it would be easier to keep this project within NetSuite given that is where the data is. But because we did not want to recreate the mess that was the previous project mentioned, we opted to build a new website complete with it's database to prevent querying the NetSuite DB too often which could be unreliable and slow. However, now we have a new website that sales staff need to constantly be on and we need to maintain it as well.
Perhaps if there were a new script type, call it an HTMLlet or a ReactLet, that allowed interaction with the webpage more freely and could receive and send data to and from NetSuite natively we could use NetSuite as a standalone application builder.

Additionally, one thing I would truly love and would make my role 1000% easier is if there were more tooling available to us! An official VS Code extension to have a SuiteScript 1.0 and 2.x/2.1 linter would make a world of difference in preventing bugs.
column10: For me, NetSuite allows me to take business data and develop applications, forms, APIs, etc. to solve business problems and simplify workflows A Software Developer's

column7:
NetSuite provides a streamlined workflow, supporting everything from budgeting to financial reporting within a single system. Different users can perform their tasks efficiently, and many reports and functions can be customized to meet specific business needs. Additionally, the system offers detailed audit logs, which help administrators identify potential weaknesses in internal controls.

Successful implementation largely depends on having the right consultants involved. Customer support is generally responsive and easy to reach. Integration with certain systems is straightforward, and we rely on NetSuite as our primary ERP system for daily operations.
column8:
The implementation requires someone with strong domain expertise in the NetSuite (NS) system, and such expertise is relatively rare in the market. Additionally, the platform often demands extensive customization—even for basic elements like invoice templates.

Regarding integration, while some software tools integrate with NS relatively easily, others present significant challenges and are not as straightforward to work with.

As for customer support, although they are generally accessible, there is room for improvement in their product knowledge. In several cases, the solutions provided have not been particularly helpful or relevant.

NetSuite offers a wide range of features, but it's surprising that some basic functionalities are still missing. For example, revaluation transactions cannot be automatically tied to departments—a simple yet valuable feature that has yet to be implemented.
column10:
NetSuite streamlines the invoicing process, ensuring consistent records throughout the entire workflow. Different users can perform their respective roles efficiently, which adds structure and clarity to operations. This has resolved many of the issues we previously faced with QuickBooks, where processes were undefined and information was scattered across systems.

The cash application feature is also user-friendly and supports various categories, improving visibility into financial activities such as bank reconciliation and reporting.

Since our business also involves hardware, NetSuite has been particularly helpful in tracking inventory across multiple locations and optimizing our supply chain processes. A Software Can Satisfy Regular Business

column7: As a Warehouse Supervisor and WMS user, I appreciate that NetSuite provides real-time inventory tracking, order management, and seamless integration with other departments. The ability to scan and allocate inventory makes warehouse operations more efficient, and the system helps maintain accurate stock levels. The customizable dashboards and reporting features also allow us to monitor performance and identify areas for improvement.
column8: While NetSuite is powerful, the WMS interface can be slow and not always intuitive, especially for warehouse tasks like wave picking, order allocation, and item reallocation. Some manual workarounds are needed to correct errors, and the system does not always commit inventory properly. Additionally, customization options can be complex and require IT support, making simple adjustments more time-consuming than necessary. Improved mobile functionality and a more user-friendly WMS workflow would greatly enhance efficiency.
column10: NetSuite helps streamline warehouse operations by providing real-time inventory visibility, order tracking, and automation of warehouse tasks. As a Warehouse Supervisor and WMS user, this benefits me in several ways Efficient but Could Use Improvements for
