column10:
Before NetSuite, you’d have finance in one tool, sales in another, and inventory in spreadsheets. That leads to blind spots, double entry, and costly mistakes.

Manual, Time Consuming Processes

Things like reconciling purchase orders, tracking shipments, or generating financial reports take forever when they’re manual.

Scaling Challenges

As the business grows, the old patchwork systems break down. NetSuite provides a single scalable platform.

column7: NetSuite is an incredibly powerful ERP system, and one of its biggest strengths is its customizability. The ability to create custom records, workflows, and SuiteScripts allows for deep personalization to fit unique business needs. This flexibility makes it an excellent tool for companies that require tailored solutions beyond out-of-the-box functionality.
column8: However, NetSuite has its downsides. The interface feels outdated, and navigating through the system can be clunky and unintuitive. Additionally, creating saved searches is more time-consuming than it should be—while they are powerful, the process is tedious, and the UI could use some improvements to make building reports and extracting data more efficient.
column10: We use NetSuite primarily to track inventory, manage orders, and handle accounting. Having everything in one place reduces manual work and ensures better accuracy in reporting and financials.

column8: I find the access levels in NetSuite problematic as they are predefined by groups and not tailored to individual roles. This forces me to switch roles multiple times a day, losing my progress in the process. The interface design and transaction logic also feel unintuitive; accessing related financial information requires navigating through multiple tabs rather than having a consolidated view. Additionally, the NetSuite apps are not straightforward in terms of pricing and deployment, making them less appealing as potential assets for the company.
column10: I find that NetSuite helps by keeping track of our operations, but the role-based access complicates my support tasks across multiple groups. Functional ERP with Room for

column7: NetSuite is a powerful platform that supports a wide range of financial operations. I appreciate all that it offers such as automating journal entries, and streamlining reporting processes. The integration capabilities with tools like Stripe and Mosaic make it easier to maintain continuity across our financial tech stack. I also find the saved searches and custom reporting features particularly useful for surfacing key data efficiently.
column8: While NetSuite is certainly robust, it can be unintuitive and overly complex for certain day-to-day tasks. The user interface isn't always user-friendly, and some functions require more manual work or custom scripting than expected for a modern ERP. I wish the searches and reports were a bit more user friendly and easier to navigate, such as what to choose to find your desired result.
column10: NetSuite is solving key problems around financial visibility and data centralization. By acting as a single source of truth for transactions, vendor payments, and reporting, it reduces the risk of errors and manual reconciliation. It also helps streamline month-end close, supports tax compliance, and integrates smoothly with tools like Stripe and Mosaic, which improves the accuracy and timeliness of forecasting and spend management.

column7:
Rapid Deployment: The environment can be spun up quickly.

SaaS Pricing Model: Subscription-based (offers flexibility but has trade-offs).

Lightweight Infrastructure: Browser-based access minimizes hardware requirements (though this has pros and cons).

Extensive Ecosystem: More SuiteApps available than most competing ERPs.

Multi-Entity Support: Native OneWorld functionality simplifies global operations.

Seamless Upgrades: Bi-annual updates at no additional cost, ensuring continuous improvement.
column8:
Lack of Accountability & Misleading Practices

A major red flag we overlooked early on was their refusal to allow meeting recordings, claiming they wanted to "protect proprietary functionality." This excuse falls flat—numerous public demos exist online. The real reason? Avoiding accountability for unmet promises. Without recorded proof of commitments, we had no recourse when deliverables fell short.

Failed Implementation & Broken Commitments
Fixed-Cost Implementation Scam: They charged upfront but delivered only half the solution, leaving us with broken workflows and no support.

Held Hostage for Basic Deliverables: To complete the original Scope of Work, they now demand payment for their "Advanced Customer Service" team (Philippines-based at $300/hour).

System & Performance Issues
Unreliable Platform: Frequent outages, sluggish browser-based performance.

Inexperienced Implementation Team: Even NetSuite’s on-shore team lacked product knowledge. Many offshore contractors had language barriers, yet we were billed premium rates.

NetSuite WMS – A Mislabeled Disaster
Not a True WMS: Missing basic functionality (e.g., real-time inventory tracking during picking/packing).

No Performance Metrics: Tracking picker/packer efficiency costs extra.

Zero Visibility: Once an item is picked, you cannot locate it (e.g., on a cart, in a tote) until shipping completes—a fundamental WMS failure.

Ship Central – A Broken Shipping Module
Inventory Discrepancies: Failed to deduct stock during shipping, leaving items stuck "in limbo."

Clunky, Error-Prone Interface:

Deviating from the workflow requires 10+ admin steps to undo.

Manual printer selection for every single shipment.

Slow Processing: 30-60 seconds "thinking" time per label, plus another 30-60 seconds to print.

Reprinting Labels is a Nightmare: No intuitive reprint option—forces digging through logs, which is impractical in paperless environments.

Final Warning
Do NOT invest in NetSuite WMS or Ship Central. Their products are incomplete, inefficient, and poorly designed—costing more in fixes and lost productivity than their value.

column7: NetSuite is a powerful and flexible ERP solution that provides the infrastructure growing businesses need to scale efficiently and operate with clarity. Its integrated modules, cloud accessibility, and real-time reporting capabilities make it a strategic asset for companies seeking to unify operations and drive long-term growth.
column8: While NetSuite offers powerful ERP capabilities, users often face several challenges. The platform has a steep learning curve, particularly for new users or non-technical staff. Implementation and customization can be costly and time-intensive, often requiring expert consultants. The user interface, while functional, is not always intuitive, and system performance can lag when handling complex reports or large datasets. Out-of-the-box reporting is somewhat limited, pushing users toward custom solutions. Additionally, managing roles and permissions can be complicated, and integrating with third-party systems may require advanced development work. Support levels vary depending on the service tier, and the licensing model can become expensive as businesses grow.

column7: NetSuite allows custom workflows, automation, and business logic using JavaScript-based SuiteScript or the visual SuiteFlow tool. Dashboards, forms, and records can be tailored by role, improving both speed and usability.NetSuite integrates well with other platforms (Salesforce, Shopify, etc.) using REST and SOAP APIs, allowing businesses to extend core functionality without overloading the system.

What I like best about NetSuite is how it's an all-in-one platform that brings together ERP, CRM, inventory, and e-commerce in one system. It really helps streamline operations and gives us real-time visibility into key business metrics.
And, 
As an admin, I appreciate how customizable and flexible NetSuite is. With SuiteScript and workflows, we can automate processes and tailor the system to fit our business needs without heavy development.
Plus, 
What I like best is NetSuite's ability to integrate with various other platforms through RESTlets, SuiteTalk, and middleware like Celigo or Boomi. It allows us to build scalable, automated processes across systems.
column8: While workflows in NetSuite allow for powerful no-code development, they can be limiting when it comes to field access and flexibility. Not all fields are supported, which can restrict more complex logic.
column10: NetSuite helps us solve the challenge of managing multiple business functions in separate systems. This has saved us time and reduced manual errors.

column8: I dislike the number of different transactions and numbering on these transactions, when all belong to the same process. Example: I have a sales order that has a SO number and the an IF with a different number, and if it has more than one IF because of pending backorders to deliver, then it is another number for that IF, and then the INV has a different number, etc. Although we can see the related transactions between each other, it takes time to track this like this. column7: I have used a number of ERP and inventory management systems in the past with varying degrees of customization options and user friendliness. Usually the more customized, the easier or complicated it can be depending on the person doing the customizing. Netsuite has seemingly endless options to customize everything from aesthetics to functionality and as long as you're willing to do most of the learning on your own, pay for extended training or pay even more for a partner or consultant to help, it's a great program. I love its potentlal and flexibility.
column8: Out of the box, barebones Netsuite will probably work for exactly nobody. You will need some levels of add on modules or scripted customization to fit your business needs, and the costs can add up very quickly. it would be nice if some of this is better explained up fron or the pricing was made more transparent, but the results in many ways speak for themselves.
column10: Netsuite will help us streamline and consilidate our many departments digital platforms. Prior to implementation we relied heavily on manually inputted putside documents and mixmatched across departments with ineffective communication within those tools. We are working towards a single platform to handle and track everything from first lead to final invoice and all the operations inbetween.

column7:
I like that Netsuite is fully integrated so there is no need for troubleshooting reconciling subledgers to the GL. I also like that Netsuite usually has links to help you navigate between linked records. 
It is generally easy to follow an audit trail to determine which user took action on a particular record. Netsuite is a flexible system
column8: Getting the data you want out of Netsuite can be tricky. Reports and searches are easily modified, but there is not an easy way to figure out field names. Unlike relational database programs, there is not a key name for each field. Selecting "Name" returns different information depending on the record type and custom fields that are entered. Once you select the correct fields, the easiest way to whittle down the data is to dump it in excel and filter/sort from there. I am considered a "SuperUser" because I spend all day working in Netsuite forms, but as a functional user my saved searches are usually not a finished result straight from Netsuite
column10:
Before Netsuite, we had separate systems for billing, AP & expenses, payroll, & reporting. 
This required significant effort to reconcile transaction integration and also resulted in a lot of batch posting to the GL, which made reconciliations more difficult and less granular.
Netsuite has removed that burden. The ARM makes revenue posting easy. We are a high volume subscription provider, Netsuite separates the invoicing from revenue recognition, which allows us to easily record revenue and deferred revenue. Netsuite

column7: NetSuite has been a game-changer for our revenue recognition process. It’s helped us automate and streamline workflows that were previously manual and time-consuming. The system has made it much easier to manage complex revenue schedules and stay compliant, saving our team valuable time and reducing the risk of errors.
column8: While NetSuite has significantly improved our processes, one challenge we've encountered is the system’s complexity. Customizing reports and setting up certain workflows can require a steep learning curve or external support. It’s a powerful platform, but getting everything tailored to our specific needs has taken more time and resources than we initially expected
column10: NetSuite is helping us solve challenges around revenue recognition, financial reporting, and managing complex data across multiple programs. Before implementing it, we relied heavily on spreadsheets and manual processes, which increased the risk of errors and took up a lot of staff time. Now, we’re able to automate key processes like revenue scheduling, improve the accuracy of our financial reports, and get real-time visibility into our financial position. Simplified, Accurate, and Efficient Revenue

column7: The best part about NetSuite is the ease of use for new users across our organization. Being able to add new users and have them up and running within a few days is a huge time save for our business. The fact that there are constantly new updates and features to the software always make me feel like we are on the cutting edge of each new type of technology.
column8: Our biggest challenge so far has been the pace of implementation. This is a lengthy process to find out exactly how to make NetSuite fit with our business, but putting in the effort up front will certainly go a long way down the road.
column10: NetSuite is helping us solve a terribly slow period close process. We were taking anywhere from 25-35 days to close a single month in our old system. We hope that with NetSuite we will be closed within 15 days after month end.

column7: NetSuite offers a robust suite of tools for finance, inventory, and order management. The cloud-based access makes it easy to use across teams, and its customizable dashboards are a big plus. It takes some time to learn, but the efficiency gains are worth it.
column8: While NetSuite is powerful, its user interface can feel outdated and less intuitive compared to modern platforms. Some processes require too many clicks, and customization often needs developer support. Additionally, customer support response times can be slow during peak hours.
column10: NetSuite helps us centralize key business operations like financials, inventory management, order processing, and reporting in one platform. This reduces manual work, improves accuracy, and provides real-time visibility into business performance, enabling faster and more informed decision-making. Flexible and Comprehensive

column7: We went live in mid-April 2022 and had a solid and engaged implementation team. Coming from multiple instances of QuickBooks, we were able to use NetSuite to fully consolidate our financials and see all of our information in one place.
column8: The drawback I have seen so far is consistently connecting and interacting with our bank accounts. The account transactions, when imported, exist only on one screen. Unlike Quickbooks, you cannot use them to post to your general ledger - you must create your own NetSuite records to match the bank records. We have also had some connection issues with required manually importing CSV files for bank transactions.

column7: NetSuite has enabled more seamless collaboration of efforts within my team structure, enabling us to easily share custom reporting, and delegate tasks like bank reconcilliations without duplicating efforts. The cloud based aspect is the main improvement over out previous system and I've found the transition, though definitely not without some difficulties, was relatively manageable.
column8: The majority of issues that I've witnessed with the new NetSuite system are related to oversights during the migration and onboarding faces of the transition. I think as a company, our needs were not effectively communicated to our NetSuite counterparts to enable us to pick up where we left off in our old system and we've paid for that with severe delays in producing financials since we've migrated. I think it is vital that the people using the software and pulling the reporting be present in the room as the customization of the platform occurs to achieve the most effective outcome and avoid the influx of CS help needed post go live.

column7:
**UPDATED** 
Having used NetSuite for several years, I've come to appreciate how it transcends the role of a traditional ERP and functions as a comprehensive business platform. NetSuite is not just a tool for accounting or operations; it's a central hub that integrates and streamlines all facets of our business activity. From financial management to supply chain operations, administrative tasks, and beyond, NetSuite offers a unified solution that empowers every department within our organization. The platform's flexibility and scalability allow us to continuously adapt to changing business needs, ensuring that everyone—whether in accounting, operations, HR, or management—derives significant value from its use. NetSuite has become an indispensable asset to our company, driving efficiency, productivity, and informed decision-making across the board.

When a small business like ours goes from Quickbooks to NetSuite, the collaboration of the team members involved go through the roof. I like that everyone has access to the info they need while specifically not having access to the info they don't.
column8: If I were to have one dislike of the system it is this, sometimes the user interface feels a little clunky. A lot of the people who use the product in our business need more of an easy-to-use interface.
column10: We've used NetSuite to save time and money on payroll processing and bill payments. We've also gained greater visibility on who is spending what and when. We have established workflows that help us manage spending / signing authority limits and spending approvals. We've upgraded our PO processes to make them more streamlined and user friendly. And the list goes on and on and on.

Our NetSuite implementation team took the time to make sure they understood our business, were available to answer questions and provide clear guidance to verfiy that we are staying on track. By implementing NetSuite, we have consolidated multiple steps of our process and are gaining greater insight into our business. Our entire team has access to the system with the goal of individuals having information at their fingertips. Also that they can monitor their inividual progresses. Our ACS team lead is very responsive and is very helpful with developing the information we need to make NetSuite a success.

This software will grow with us very nicely as we grow - we will not need to transition to a new system as we grow and expand.

column7: Pretty flexible for an ERP system and it is working most time.
column8:
1. They dont let us record any meeting, this is causing a very painful implementation.
2. They dont let us choose my calendly calendar, instead they will send you a time a link and I have to do it manually.
3. Support team will call you right about 4:30-5:00pm, just when about to go home for the day.
4. Aggressive updates timeline.
5. Broken updates and downstream implications.
6. If my support ticket got referred to a different person, I will have to explain all over again. I have been referred to multiple people on several different ocassions. Very frustrating!
7. If they dont have any answer, my ticket goes to "product enhancement", which is technically goes to a backburner that will never be fixed.
8. They keep saying it will get faster (during sales pitch), and also during implementation. After implementation, they are pointing the finger to either your internet, your browser, and not them. It is slow as heck compared to our cloud ERP.
column10:
Nothing.Netsuite is actually causing us more problems and more money. Be wary when you have the sales pitch meeting with them. They keep saying that you do not need an IT person during sales pitch, their idea is to move all of your IT resources to them and paying them double, triple, or even quadruple. Their ACS (after sales) technical support is somewhat competent (depending on the team), but they are very slow. I had to fire our first ACS team. They are winning the 2023 & 2024 slowest achivement to complete a project in my book. However, I think they are slow by design. The slower they work, the more amount of money that are spent because they are on an hourly basis. They will include useless (non contributing) people into a meeting, then they will charge you on the hours for them to stay and breathe in the meeting. I can only applaud CPQ team (I think they bought out Verenia), they are honest and very good. I have no issue with their CPQ team (their existence made me add 3 more stars from 0 star). As an administrator, doing roles maintenance because switching roles and screen is super painful. They dont allow you to be in more than 1 role at a particular time, not even in a different browser.

If you have a lot of money to waste and you like your project to extend to years, then NetSuite is the one for you. From my experience, there are other better alternatives out there and I personally regret moving to NetSuite. Money sucking vortex - It is a win for

column8: NetSuite UI is pretty out fashioned. It needs to be redesigned like Salesforce. It slow as well sometimes to edit one record we have to wait for 30 seconds to load the website. column7: The freedom to generate reports and be 100% creative is such a huge benefit for what we do at Spraywell. This flexibility provides us with every opportunity to review data in clusters, groups, or standalone. The ability to integrate with all of our other softwares is very helpful as well. For the most part, Netsuite is easy to use. There are complex features that require the user to fully understand it's capability and that is okay.
column8: The only thing I dislike about Netsuite is the lack of training on scripting for Netsuite. I know that if we were to have training on how to "speak" to Netsuite, and build more functional reports, we would be able to utilize more of the platform. Unfortunately, we have to pay outside services to build certain reports and functions to be able to utilize Netsuite how we want to. This can become an associated cost with keeping Netsuite as our ERP system.
column10: Netsuite is solving many of our problems by acting as the brain of our core operations. Hosting everything within Customer accounts, all the way to our 60,000 SKUs. You can be as broad or granular as you want within each record and report and we love this feature. Spraywell

column7: We are able to speed up our billing and revenue recognition processes. We are able to customize the experience to our entity's needs.
column8: The audit log in saved searches and SuiteBilling change orders don't always timestamp the details that are changed. Custom objects and fields may not populate in system notes with the details of what items were changed (i.e. new value vs old value). The new realeased features of add-on's don't allow for CVS uploading, which require manual work. Additionally, one area of pain is subscription renewal. When renewing a subscription that may have past change orders, the system recalculates the TCV and does a catch-up for prior period change orders. We want to be able to bypass this so that changes can be recognized prospectively and we can keep one subscription per contract (unless there are substantial changes).
column10: NetSuite allows us to send out billing in a timely process and recognize revenue according to GAAP and othere regulation agency standards.

column8: NetSuite's basic support and even premium support leave alot to be desired. As an 8 year user of the product I always hoped that their training would be broadened and options not be so coin operated (10K for training portal). One of the best ways to grow the brand is allow alot of people to learn the product.
column10: NetSuite is solving the universal problem most businesses have which is multiple versions of the truth when it comes to numbers. With NetSuite, all reports and searches are based off the same dataset and when it comes to accurate and timely reporting this is critical. Having all customer data being pathed through one product allows for minimal issues with missing data and incomplete forms. Since NetSuite supports data import, we do manual and API based import of data for sales (ex. leads).

column7: Its ease of use and the way everything is seamlessly integrated allow users to manage multiple processes from a single platform, optimizing time and resources while reducing operational complexity.
column8: NetSuite operates entirely as a cloud-based platform, which means it requires an active internet connection to access and use its features. The lack of offline modes can be seen as a limitation in certain scenarios, such as when users are in areas with limited or unreliable internet connectivity.

column7: Our current system is QuickBooks, so needless to say just about anything would be an upgrade. right now what I am seeing is the reporting is much more robust, which is amazing. The customized AP approval routing that we were able to install, is perfect for a small company. And the potential possibilities for upgrades and additions with other suite apps exciting to think about.
column8: Right now, I feel as though the ACS training and people involved with scheduling and pricing and things like that are not all on the same page. We've had a few issues with that part of it. It would be nice to know that when you sign a contract and put dates to your implementation, that things could happen and they will work with you on your timeline and extend any kind of training or resources that they initially offered with the contract. And although there are a lot of apps that can be added to the whole suite, it also seems a bit cumbersome that things are so à la cart, as opposed to part of Package.

column7: I love that you can navigate through transactions from start to finish. In my case, working as an accountant, if I am reviewing an account, I can go through several clicks to get to it, and then to the integrations. I love the configurable dashboard and the way you can work across multiple countries.
column8: There are things that are not standardized between companies. I have used NetSuite in two companies, and there are things like mandatory fields that were required in one but not in the other. I know this is an admin configuration, but it should be a standard or have an option to automatically request these changes. This is the same when ordering a transaction: in one company, the line number appeared, and here it does not. Additionally, when creating a report, it seems so complex to understand the fields since they seem to be repeated. It would be clearer if we could extract fields, for example, the type of product entered from the creation of a journal.
column10: NetSuite solves several problems by providing a comprehensive view of transactions from start to finish, which is especially beneficial in my role as an accountant. Its ability to navigate through various transactions with just a few clicks simplifies the process of reviewing accounts and integrations. The configurable dashboard enhances efficiency by allowing for customization to suit specific needs, and its capability to operate seamlessly across multiple countries is invaluable for global operations. Although there are some inconsistencies in configurations between companies, the overall functionality and flexibility of NetSuite significantly improve workflow and productivity. Great software for Medium-Large

column7: Netsuite is the gold standard for accounting software so everyone uses it, there is a lot of help online if you do not know how to do something. They way you can format transaction searches is better than any other program I have ever used, they can be so tailored to exactly what you need. Financial statement presentation is clean and the way everything is linked makes sense. Almost every other software any depatment uses can be integrated with Netsuite. Once you know how to work your instance of netsuite, you can pretty much work anyone's version of netsuite. Customer Support has always answered any inquiries we had the same day.
column8: I don't think netsuite has kept up with the advances in technology, it seems like some things can be a little more intuative and less manual, have not seen real changes in a decade and it's starting to feel "old" like Great Plaines. Also, I think that consulting with actual accountants that use the service would help make things a little easier for running reports, providing information for other departments, proactively identifying accruals, etc.
column10: Netsuite makes it very easy to see all of the transactions by whatever parameter you would like to search, be it customer name, expense account, dates, departments, etc. This is beneficial because when someone outside of the accounting department asks for ANYTHING, whether it be spend, number of invoices, payments received whatever, I can very efficiently get that information. column8:
I am writing to express my extreme dissatisfaction with the service and support provided by NetSuite over the past year. Unfortunately, my experience has been fraught with numerous issues, ultimately leading me to decide not to renew our contract.

From the outset, the implementation of NetSuite was handled incorrectly, causing significant disruptions to our business operations. Whenever we encountered an issue, the standard resolution offered was to purchase an additional module, which felt more like a sales strategy than a genuine effort to resolve our problems.

Moreover, the pricing structure for essential features such as payroll and profit and loss by jobs was not made clear upfront, leading to unexpected costs that strained our budget. Throughout our time with NetSuite, I found that no one seemed to have the necessary expertise to fix the issues we faced. Despite numerous requests to speak with supervisors, I was consistently told that our problems could be solved by spending more money, which was both frustrating and unacceptable.

Compounding these issues was the frequent turnover of our account manager, who changed four times within the year. This lack of continuity meant that no one had a clear understanding of our account history or ongoing issues, resulting in repeated explanations and a general lack of progress in resolving our concerns.

It is evident that the primary focus was on receiving payment rather than providing effective solutions and support. This approach has left us with a system that does not meet our needs and has caused considerable disruption to our operations.

Given these circumstances, I cannot justify renewing our contract with NetSuite. I hope this feedback will be taken seriously and lead to improvements in your customer support and service delivery for the benefit of future clients.

column7: I think one of the most helpful features of NetSuite is its unified platform. It really makes a big difference that all our departments can use just the one system to get everything done, from ERP, CRM, e-commerce, financials and HR. Also the custimization that is available to us has been vital. We have been able to scale the system exactly the way we need to run our business most efficiently.
column8: Cost can be a factor at times especially when we need to impliment an new addon or business process. There is also a bit of a learning curve just because how much we have customized our specific system. Also the dependency on internet connectivity. because it is cloud-based if we were to lost internet in the office we would have very heavy disruptions and downtime until we can connect again.
column10:
It has helped with real-time visibility in business management. We can track our financial metrics and inventory levels easily and in real time with customizable dashboards and reports. This has helped us make informed decisions in a more prompt way. 
NetSuite has also helped with manual/error prone processes by automating alot of our production/dispatching processes which i believe has helped minimize errors improving efficiency and producivity.

column7: Netsuite CRM and ARP are outstanding! These sectors are excellent in terms of pick, pack, and ship as well as inventory management and customer support. This all-in-one business management solution is strong and adaptable, providing real-time visibility, user-friendliness, and first-rate customer service. The most appealing aspect of NetSuite to me is its versatility and adaptability. It can be tailored to meet your specific requirements. Similar to assembling Lego blocks, you begin with the fundamentals and tack on modules as your company expands. Because it's cloud-based, you can access it from anywhere at any time. I also appreciate that Netsutie allows for different stages of a company's growth and integrates with a lot of external tools or apps for a better user interface. Without a doubt, NetSuite provides everything you need from an accounting system
column8: Even with all of NetSuite's benefits, there are a few drawbacks to take into account: Learning how to utilize it efficiently takes time, especially for inexperienced users. The slowness of the system can be annoying, and getting around it can be challenging. To set it up and resolve problems, you frequently require support and technical assistance. These features make it challenging to use and may reduce the amount of work you are able to do.

column7: NetSuite is a powerful ERP that allows businesses to consolidate processes across the organization into a single application (e.g. financials, sales/CRM, inventory, eCommerce, payroll, etc.). There are limitless ways to customize NetSuite for your specific use case, and it can evolve with your company's growth.
column8: Because of how versatile the NetSuite platform is, allowing for constant optimization, there can be a fair amount of maintenance/ongoing effort associated with managing your environment. Business leaders must realize this is a technology investment, and there will be future costs associated with the solution. It's not just a "set it and forget it" type of scenario because there will always be improvements that can/should be made to keep up with your users and business.
column10: Instead of having processes spread across disperate applications (Saleforce, Shopify, QuickBooks, Zendesk, Office) we can consolidate many things under the NetSuite environment, facilitating a unique user experience across many departments and enhansing company-wide reporting. Versatile

column7: NetSuite is like a chameleon for businesses—it can change its colors to fit any size or type of company. Whether you're just starting out or already a big player, NetSuite can adjust to what you need and keep up as your business grows. You can tweak it a lot to match exactly how you work without needing to be a coding expert. This means NetSuite stays useful no matter how much your business changes and grows.
column8: Putting NetSuite into action can be tough, especially for businesses with their own special ways of doing things or old systems in place. Getting it set up and working right at the start needs people who really know their stuff, and it can take a while to get the hang of using it, especially if you're new to the platform. If you're not careful, all this complexity can drag out the time it takes to get NetSuite up and running and end up costing you more than you bargained for.
column10: NetSuite streamlines business tasks by bringing everything into one place. From finances to customer management, it handles it all, making life simpler. With real-time data and automated processes, you spend less time on paperwork and more on growing your business. Plus, it's customizable, so it fits your company like a glove. Ease Business

column7: The product is very robust for sales and financial operations. Once the processes are worked out for your business, the Orders to Cash and Procure to Pay processes are detailed and capture substantial amounts of data for susbequent reporting and analysis.
column8:
The implementation process was inadequate. There wasn't sufficient analysis of the existing workflows by the NetSuite subject matter experts to determine (a) how NetSuite could accommodate existing workflows or (b) if existing workflows needed to change to accomodate NetSuite processes or perhaps leverage NetSuite features and functions. 
Similarly, the training was lacking. It was more a demonstration of default NetSuite features rather than training on the product as installed and configured in our environment.
While additional training and support is available for a fee, the basic implementation and training need to be strenghtened to support a successful initial adoption.
column10: We were looking for an ERP that had specific features and functionaly associated with advertising revenues as a Media company. We also have several subsidiaries, and some deal with product/inventory sales, so a robust inventory costing/managment module was important. The multi-subsidiary structure required comprehensive intercompany processes that included elimination entries and a comprehensive set of fincial reports with roll-up. NetSuite provided the features and functionality we were looking for. The product is great.

column7: Having everything in one spot makes my day so much easier. I can pull financial reports while also checking on how CRM leads are doing for Sales. column8: Netsuite can be incredibly challenging to implement without the correct team or without a strong enough background knowledge. Migration from another ERP to NS (ex Fishbowl to Netsuite) is significantly easier than moving a team from Quickbooks to Netsuite to manage inventory. Netsuite as a software is not exactly easy to use, and there are certain little things (such as clicking cancel on a line after adding an item to a transaction) or that nothing autosaves so sometimes all progress is completely lost if you miss a single field. It is also extremely challenging to teach those who are not technologically inclined to use Netsuite on a day to day basis

column7: NetSuite allows for customizations to be made to improve process efficiency. It allows you to create custom fields, customize exisiting forms to better suite your needs, and develop workflows to introduce automation into your process. Combine this with the fact that it functions entirely in the cloud and you have a solution that can easily adapt to your businesses every need. Since it is very widely used, there is also a robust support system - from other NetSuite customers to NetSuite's official customer support.
column8: Currently NetSuite's reporting is a bit limited. They are working to address this with NSAW, but within native NetSuite your options are limited to fairly simple data aggregations.

column7: As a small business we were looking to upgrade from Quickbooks so we could have a more integrated view of the business end-to-end. NetSuite had the features were were looking for and thought it would be the solution we needed based on the sales calls and promises made during the sales process, including inital pricing and incentives.
column8:
Everything after the sales process completed. I have never written a product or software review, but feel compelled to warn small business owners about NetSuite so they don't wind up in the same situation.

In my 22 years as a small business owner I can easily say this is the BIGGEST mistake I have ever made and by far the worst purchasing decision. Unless you are large enough to have a full time, very technical resource completely dedicated to the implementation process, and time to allow your full team to completely self train on the system you will have problems, no matter what they promise you during the sales process.

We dropped NetSuite after the 1st year as we STILL WERE NOT FULLY IMPLEMENTED 1 year into the process (and had to pay the full license fees for that full year even though we didn't have a fully functional system the entire time!) The package we paid for the implementation process provided horrible support. It was an inexperienced team of two people who always had to get back to us with any questions we had (2-7 days for an answer) - never knew the system beyond just repeating what was in the generic training videos (which are horrible training videos) and processes. Zero project management, we were just told 3 weeks before our 'implementation term' ended that their time is up and even though our system wasn't implemented they are done - no warning or notice. No reminder at each 'monthly' meeting that they only have x weeks left of their support (yes, once per month is there connection point to get you implemented, that's it). Only options at that point were to pay more fees to add on consulting packages - which still took another 3 months just to get the payroll module (another up charge) implemented.

Renewal pratices are unethical. No renewal contract was provided until 15 days before inital 1 year contract ended - thinking that in such a short time we would be stuck and have to renew as there would be no time to flip elsewhere. I said no, I won't renew. We had been having to 'double enter' everything into QBs the entire year since our NS system was never fully implemented - and we decided we would rather just stay with QBs. The account rep then told me well too bad, in your original contract it states you have to provide me with 30 day notice if you will not renew so you have to renew whether you want to or not. I argued how could I provide 30 days notice that I don't want to sign a renewal contract when my renewal contract wasn't provided until 15 days prior to end of term - no response from rep at all. Instead, they just automatically renewed me against my rejection and I received the renewal invoice and was charged the full next year amount. They were FORCING my renewal even though she knew I was incredibly unhappy with the system. It was the biggest 1 year $100k+ mistake I ever made ($40k to them, easily another $60k in internal labor costs) - walked away and will never have anything to do with NetSuite again. 

If you are a small business without full time staff to completely manage the implementation effort, run away and don't get sucked into the sales promises - none of them panned out. What I projected as a $30k per year expense (which was a stretch for my small business) based on the sales team's promises quickly turned into so much more to actually get everything I needed once implementation was rolling. And the unethical renewal practices are completely not okay.
column10: None. I dropped NetSuite after the first year as it was not meeting our needs and was not able to do what we were promised during the sales process, without also paying for additional modules and additional consulting time to customize and confirgure the system. After reading reviews about the renewal prices radically and quickly escalating to keep this behemoth, I decided it was best to move on.

column7: Netsuite is user friendly. Setting up ad-hoc reports, static reports, and drilling down into the data on the general ledger doesn't require hours and hours of training. Many things are self explanitory. Implementation isn't especially complicated compared to other systems, especially. We have integrated this with several of our clients in the past and migrated their data to Netsuite, and all have been satisfied.
column8: It has its caveats just like any other reporting tool. It does take some time to learn how to set it up as an admin, and a bit of training for the users to understand, but what FP&A tool doesn't?
column10: In FP&A, in many cases, a lot of reporting tools/templates take quite a lot of time to populate and we get bogged down doing manual work, populating and crunching numbers. This is especially the case when performing ad-hoc, deep dive, drilldowns to find specific performance drivers. Netsuite allows for the automation of such tools & reports and allows for very easy data investigation. This saves our department a lot of time which is then being put to better use, actually performing analyses and interpreting data to make better decisions. Great FP&A

column7: I like that you can customize almost every business process/workflow in NetSuite to fit your unique business needs. They provide methods for both technical and non technical people to make these customizations.
column8: Their pricing and renewal strategy always leaves us feeling like we aren't getting the value for our money. After being a customer for 6 years, our pricing was increased by 130% on our renewal all while losing features.
column9: All of our systems are integrated with Netsuite. This allows us to get a full picture of our business instead of having to look at data from many different platforms. This allows us to make intelligent business decisions. Netsuite has also allowed us to easily scale our business, adding new modules and functionality as needed. Everything you need in an

column7: NetSuite is completely customizable, if you have the knowledge and use-case requirements to complete the customizations. Without customizations, workflows, or scripting the out of the box UI can be clunky and busy. Once you learn the ways to customize NetSuite the UE gets a lot better.
column8: Depending on your industry, a lot of what NetSuite offers out of the box will need to be customized or will need a third-party suiteapp or suitebundle (additional cost with that vendor) that does the process better then NetSuite. Implementation will be a bear no matter who you are. You will most likely need a GOOD third-party (charging T&M) implementation team to help you implement, and you will need an internal project manager that knows your data and processes really well.
column10: We are currently using NetSuite with other third parties for Corporate Accounting and Finance (NS), Purchase Orders (NS), Revenue Rec (NS), Fixed Asset/Lease management and accounting (third-party in NS), Intercompany accounting (third-party in NS), and Close management (third-party in NS). Ongoing implementations include Billing for 40k+ customers (third-party in NS) and Inventory Managment (NS). Great for Mid-Size

column7: NetSuite is user friendly and highly customizable ERP solution that allows generating various reports by slicing and dicing the data different ways. It has options to setup custom segments that can be customized for various businesses. The dashboards are easily customizable. The customer support team is very easily approachable and is very knowledgeable.
column8:
The development team is way behind what other competitors offer, the request for enhancements some times takes years and the basic reporting and automations are missing. For example, the consolidated balance sheet can not be generated with Accounting period columns and the enhancement request is open for over 3 years now. This particular enhancement has been requested by various accounting firms and client.

The implementation process varies based on the client and implementation partners. The SuiteSuccess bundle is very easy to implement but a manufacturing company's implementation can take 6 -12 months and is expensive.

column10: Prior to NetSuite we had a cumbersome process to build financial reports. Many of our accounting processes have been automated with the help of NetSuite such as bank account reconciliation and prepaid expense amortization. Unmatched Reporting

column7:
My favorite feature in NetSuite is the freedom to customize our environment to suit our business requirements and the needs of key stakeholders. The system's reporting, searching and analysis tools are second to none and have transformed the way our organization operates. 

Our implementation was very smooth thanks to our excellent preferred partner (AppFiciency). They guided us through the entire process and executed a penny perfect implementation. 

Our organization has grown tremednously over the last five years, with daily average users totalling about 150, so the frequency of use in our environment is quite high. NetSuite's customer support has been a life saver on several occasions. I highly recommend paying for premium support. You will feel well served by the investment.

We make frequent use of third party integrations and the ease with setting these up is quite good for enterprise software.

All in all, NetSuite is the class leading software for a reason!
column8:
NetSuite is class-leading software and the incredible capabilities come with a price. NetSuite is a major annual cost for our company but it has been well worth the investment. 

Our end users are typically intimidated by the UI when they first join the organization, so from that perspective I would rank ease of use a little lower than consumer grade software.
column10: Integrating our order to cash, procure to pay and project to delivery workflows in one system has unlocked tremendous value for our organization. Accomplishing all of these core business processes in one system has been a huge efficiency gain and provides us with key data that can be reported on for planning purposes. The size and scale of our business demanded a transition away from legacy accounting software five years ago. At the time, we had multiple software tools to handle expense management, project budgeting, project management and core accounting. NetSuite offered us the opportunity to bring all of those processes under the umbrella of one system which unlocked administrative efficiencies and the power of consolidated data analysis and modeling. column7: In my experience, NetSuite offers a comprehensive suite of integrated business applications, which has been incredibly helpful in streamlining our operations. The cloud-based platform provides the flexibility we need, allowing us to access our data and work from anywhere with an internet connection. One of the standout features for us has been the ease of use, and high level of customization, which has allowed us to tailor the software to fit our specific needs and workflows perfectly. We implemented it ourselves and were live within 12 weeks. Additionally, the reporting and analytics tools have provided valuable insights into our business operations, aiding in data-driven decision-making. The scalability of NetSuite is also a major plus, as it has grown with our company and adapted to our changing requirements over time.
column8: While NetSuite has been beneficial for our business, some businesses may think they're too small for an ERP system. Since the software offers a wide array of functionality that you cannot get with basic accounting tools and CRM, the initial investment may be an issue for very small companies however, there is an outsourcing model available for consideration until your business is ready to implement the system internally. This will allow early adoption as your business grows and scales.
column10:
From a CRM perspective, NetSuite is solving the problem of fragmented customer data and disjointed processes. It provides a centralized platform where we can manage all aspects of our customer relationships. This includes tracking interactions, managing leads and opportunities, and analyzing customer behavior and preferences.

NetSuite's CRM functionality allows us to have a holistic view of our customers. We can see their purchase history, communication history, and any ongoing interactions with our sales and support teams. This unified view enables us to provide personalized and timely responses to customer inquiries and needs.

Moreover, NetSuite's CRM helps automate many manual tasks, such as lead assignment, follow-ups, and nurturing campaigns. This automation ensures that no potential opportunity slips through the cracks, and it frees up our team to focus on building meaningful relationships with our customers.

Overall, NetSuite's CRM capabilities have improved our efficiency and effectiveness in managing customer interactions, leading to stronger relationships and better customer satisfaction. It has also provided valuable insights into customer behavior and preferences, allowing us to tailor our offerings and communications to better meet their needs.

column8:
Slow implementation
Implementation Team leaves your account with valuable experience after go-live, transitioning over to a less experienced Account Manager. 
High Turnover of staff leads to support that often have little experience with your business. During implementation, this creates setbacks as new staff learns your business. 
Notification Limitations for Customers/Vendor transactions. One default email field per record. 
Limitations on some rather basic functions that require upgraded or third party services to maximize performance: Customer Billing, Accounts Receivable, Accounts Payable, Shipping.

column10: NetSuite is helping us with orders going out. If customers have past due balances, NS catches that and will not allow that customer to receive new orders until Current. column7: I've been working with ERPs for over 30 years now and I've used lots of different software. NetSuite was new for me with this job. But I've been able to pick it up quickly. Between the tutorials, the seminars and the availability of material on YouTube, I've been able to get my job done, and even improve a few processes. The interface is relatively easy to adapt to, and the functions are broad. You can do pretty much anything you need to do with relatively straightforward processing, but you can also make the system enrich your financials. Building dashboards to keep track of your most important KPI's and provde non-finance manager bosses with the info they need at a glance. I'm really glad I got the chance to learn Netsuite.
column8: The naming conventions can be a little obtuse, but this comes with any new system you try to learn.
column10: Our inventory is very complex and NetSuite is able to provide the detail we need to deliver the right product, stock the right materials, and provide financials the company needs. Very Good

column7: It is essentially an interface to the database that makes it really easy to customize and import, search, and report on the data you enter. As an accoutning platform, users who are migrating from a QuickBooks style systems will have a harder time as it is not a comprehensively detailed bookkeeping system out of the box - it can be customized to get it there. As an ERP, it makes the often dysfunctional process of cross departmental handoffs really seamless and easy.
column8: A lot of its components are developed as third party apps which you would need to purchase, install/integrate separately. For small to mid sized companies, would highly recommend working with a third party consultantcy who are technically savvy and can help add scripts and workflows etc. during the implementation period. Also recommend to get most of the customizations done during this period as the post launch support team is not as responsive and tech savvy as the implementation team. It often takes more than a few status check emails to get a response back and projects take a very long time to turn around.

column7:
Comprehensive Solution: NetSuite is an all-in-one business software that includes ERP, CRM, PSA, and E-commerce capabilities, making it comprehensive and versatile. This means businesses can manage almost every aspect of their operation within one integrated system.

Cloud-based: NetSuite is cloud-based, which means it can be accessed anytime, anywhere. This provides significant flexibility for businesses with remote or international teams.

Scalability: NetSuite can be scaled to accommodate business growth. Whether a company is a startup, midsize or a large enterprise, NetSuite can tailor to its needs.

Real-time Analytics: NetSuite offers real-time dashboards and reporting functionality, allowing businesses to monitor their progress and adapt to changes quickly.

Customization: NetSuite can be tailored to meet the unique needs of each business. This can involve adjusting the platform's modules, interfaces, or workflows to perfectly fit a company's operational requirements.

Automation: NetSuite allows for automation of certain tasks, which could save time and reduce human error.
column8:
Customer Service: Some users have had difficulties working with the NetSuite team 

Occasional Technical Issues: There are instances where users have experienced technical issues, such as not being able to post an invoice, although it seems this issue was quickly addressed

Complex Customizations: Some users found that NetSuite's extensive customization can make the product harder to learn and use, specifically in the context of NetSuite Reporting in Excel
column10: Reporting and Business Intelligence: NetSuite provides real-time access to live financial data to help businesses make data-driven decisions. Reporting and Business Intelligence: NetSuite provides real-time access to live financial data to help businesses make data-driven decision.

Netsuite has not been working on anything I have seen to improve their WMS system and make it work as well/efficiently as other competitors! A WMS is the backbone of a true "soup to nuts" ERP, this is where the rubber meets the road and fulfilment actualy happens. Other NetSuite customers are using compeitors WMS systems which causes extra issues, workarounds, expenses, etc... Provide one seemless product instead of looking for competitors to give you a complete product. 

The WMS is barebones and not at all on par with cheaper competitor products. Look to mirror EasyWMS if you want an easy to use, powerful, robust, and all in one solution... Make it work their way and you would have a competitive product. Good middle of the road

NetSuite is highly configurable to meet your business needs, so it should be flexible enough to meet most business needs... if you have the time and money to spend on a certified administrator. You should plan on getting professional help setting up and maintaining.

With that in mind, the record keeping and saved searches are fairly intuitive. It definitely gets the job done, and does its functions well.
column8:
NetSuite feels a bit clunky and isn't incredibly user-friendly, so if you use it, keep in mind you'll have to do a bit more training on it than some other programs. Further, sometimes it can be pretty slow to pull reports and saved searches depending on how wide your criteria is, or just slow to create and save records in general (which isn't really a unique problem to NetSuite). 

One thing I would like improvement from NetSuite on is support. NetSuite doesn't have a terribly good help desk, so getting new things set up or learning something new will likely require forking out for consultants.

column7:
The built-in domain expertise around accounting, inventory management, and financial reporting is outstanding. 

The platform also is also very integration-friendly. Inbound setup is simple for custom applications and outbound connections are limitless using their scripting platform. Rolling out custom web services is quick with Suitelets, and the third-party connector ecosystem is pretty strong.

It is significantly more agile and easier to manage than traditional, large-scale ERPs.
column8:
The most challenging thing about NetSuite is customization and configuration management. The platform is very much configurable, and it's scripting extension capabilities are wide-open. However, it lacks key features to fit into a traditional change management scheme. Unlike some of its low-code competitors, it does not expose all of its configuration knobs and switches as externalizable metadata. So, while we can easily manage script versions in traditional source code fashion, we cannot similar connect the configuration change dots. So, when a developer addresses a customization story, we can associate script changes in source control the same way as we do our bespoke applications, but all the related (and necessary) declarative changes, such as custom fields, workflow definitions live in NetSuite alone. We can deploy scripts using automation, but config changes have to be applied by manually by administrators and history tracked with our own config tracking documentation scheme.

The other biggest issue, perhaps more of a missed opportunity, is the CRM module. It's poor user experience for Sales staff, and the relatively weak case management features make it difficult to support - which is a shame because having the CRM and ERP on the same platform solves a lot of big ticket challenges for us.
column10: NetSuite (as any ERP) manages the core business processes and workflows of the organization. It drives procurement, inventory / warehouse management, sales, and of course, finance + accounting. In terms of benefits (relative to competitive options), it's hard to do an A-B comparison. However, the platform's stability has really made the SaaS benefits real for our IT organization. The flexibility has allowed us to be highly responsive to our business stakeholders in supporting their custom workflows and desired automations. column7: NetSuite is a very powerful tool and can provide automation and reporting to a very detailed level.
column8:
NetSuite is not very user friendly at all. It often feels like you need an programming course to make changes to a report, and the bespoke nature of the software makes it impossible to utilise the help centre. All of the add ons are great, but it's an additional cost to an already very expensive software (which require businesses to have an IT support provider for it). No automated bank feeds or integrated payroll solution in 2023 (at least in Australia) is absolutely awful. And it is very hard to hire someone with NetSuite experience making the new user experience process take a lot longer than with other software. 

Although NetSuite can do finance tasks, it was not built with a finance mind, so it's particularly difficult for finance teams, and it would be great to see improvements in experience for Accounting/Finance users.

column7: Complete cloud based system requiring limited to no IT support. As the CFO of our small to mid-sized company I am able to provide the on-going support for our NetSuite ERP solution.
column8: I am not thrilled with NetSuite's pricing model. They offer deep discounts off list price to get started with your subscription and then over-time back-off the discount , with the goal of you eventually having to pay the full list subscription price - typically increase is 10% annually plus a reduction in your discount (i.e. start at 50%, reduced to 45%, 40%, etc). The general argument is that they are continuously improving their solution and there is no overt charge for upgrades, beyond adjustments to their list price and discounts given. Regardless, the value (price vs. quality) is certainly there. Biggest concern is now that Oracle owns NetSuite, their pricing model will become incomprehensible and they will find a way to make you pay for the same solution over and over again by repacking and calling it something else, like Fusion.
column9: Multi-company capabilities was a big benefit to our business. NetSuite allows you to easily consolidate multiple entities, making accounting breeze. Also, the Global Search and Saved Searches and robust reporting allow you to generate about any kind of report you may need, as well as email alerts, and easy distribution via email. Saved Searches and Report writing are fairly easy. Great dashboard capabilities allow you to monitor your key performance indicators and also provide quick access to reports and information you need and use most. All very configurable to meet your specific needs/requirements, both as a company and individually. Scripts sit on top of the base software and are seldom affected by upgrades, making it easy to customize NetSuite to meet your specific business needs. Also like the rich unit of measure conversion capabilities that allow you to purchase in one unit of measure and stock or sell in another (i.e. purchase in metric tons or Kilograms and sell in pounds). Feature-rich cloud based ERP Solution -
column10: NetSuite is a great solution for a growing company. We had 15 employees for our primary operating units (3) and recently purchased another that doubled the number of employees to 30. We were able to implement NetSuite for the new company to centralize and integrate all the accounting transactions (A/R, A/P, Financial Reporting, etc.) within a month's time. We maintain no IT Staff to support our ERP system. I would highly recommend NetSuite as a robust solution that is capable of meeting the needs of small, medium sized and large companies. Whether it is accounting, warehouse management, manufacturing, CRM, HR, Payroll, or online storefront/website, NetSuite can provide you with what you need for your business at a reasonable price. I look at it this way, our annual subscription price is just over a third the cost of a dedicated IT Professional and it provides employees with a robust time-saving fully integrated technology tool to manage your business.

column7: We have had significant success working with implementers and consultants who are directly involved with Oracle. They are very knowledgeable and easy to work with. I would strongly recommend to the originating communities to succeed in achieving. Although it is not very agile compared to certain tools in your area, the NetSuite platform capability is good and there are no problems in its regular use. From accounting controls to ERP and CRM, there isn't much that NetSuite can't handle.
column8: One of the nice things about the system is the automatic, periodic renewal of the system. You can't find a date and find that there are six different versions of your program. Despite this, despite the fact that it has provided us with enormous development periods mentioned above, the training and documentation for new resources are several months behind schedule. We have observed multiple instances where a happy new feature was not sufficiently documented or had limited documentation, which was not sufficient to explain the new feature. Livelihood is not very good, however it sometimes operates in different time zones and in different categories. On certain occasions it may be challenging to solve a difficulty, however to be fair, we have not had a complication that has not dissipated.
column10: We are currently using Oracle's NetSuite product to enhance the totality of the organization's features. Accounting, order management, inventory control, purchasing, supply planning, and all the major day-to-day tasks in the company are being executed by this web-based program. In my current company, since 2018, NetSuite was implemented to replace an old and traditional program, this program is composed of several software components that interact with each other and result in an exchanged information.

As a result, integrating world-class SaaS services into your workflow via NetSuite promises huge efficiency gains.
column8: I find NetSuite to be incredibly slow and expensive, putting it out of reach for many users. column7: The flexibility in customization and the solid performance make Netsuite an unrivaled leader in full-service ERP systems. My advice to anyone thinking of implementing NetSuite is if you want a smooth implementation and excellent user adoption then take the time to do discovery interviews with each employee. The person that does the job every day is the most knowledgeable about the process. As a multiple company user and implementer of NetSuite, I know firsthand how skipping this step in favor of interviewing 2-3 people as your baseline for configuration is going to yield a troublesome result. Make sure your implementation teams interview all of the little guys about their day-to-day activities. This single step empowers that worker to feel heard and in turn they will openly embrace the change in systems instead of fighting it.
column8: The downside is the learning curve and lack of training on the front end before Going Live. Users can sometimes take a class two or three weeks before using the system and forget everything they learned by the time it is time to use it. Take a day for a dry run on the weekend with fake transactions in the sandbox. Practice makes perfect! The investment into a fantastic system and skimping when it comes to training is foolish. If you are going to invest, then invest in training the people responsible for the day-to-day operations in using the new tool and understanding how it impacts the transactions that follow.
column10: NetSuite can solve many problems depending on each business. I have seen eCommerce businesses thrive using automated marketing campaigns. NetSuite is the most robust flexible ERP system

column7:
Customization is quick and easy for most of NetSuite.

Adding new features via SuiteScript is fairly intuitive and doesn't require re-inventing the wheel. Very nice APIs that are fun to work with. All the different parts of NetSuite work together really well, and for the most part, prevent inadvertently creating brittleness and dependency messes while still allowing us to do almost anything we can imagine.

When we need to add a feature or functionality to NetSuite, or integrate it with another system, then we almost always can with no additional cost, and we can usually do it in the way that works best for us, instead of having to re-structure our business around the new feature's canned solution.
column8:
Permissions are often unclear as to what are all the things they affect.

Search queries and similar will often take an order of magnitude longer to execute than equivalent queries in other systems.

Demand planning does not take perishable inventory's expiration dates into account, and it is unclear what the need is across multiple locations when ordering items.

SuiteCommerce does not allow us to use different units of measure in the web store, forces us to use only the primary sales unit. We manage our sales and purchases in 1,000s but customers need to buy in full truck load pallets, which are different sizes (quantity of pieces per pallet) depending on what kind of items are on the pallets. This inflexibility has been a major roadblock for us being able to usefully implement SuiteCommerce Standard.

It's also very hard to find knowledge about customizing and troubleshooting SuiteCommerce Standard.
column10:
Provides a central clearinghouse for our transactions and data. All our business teams can come to the same place to get the information they need, even when they have very different roles.

We are finding more and more that we don't need to purchase and maintain multiple different systems (still do on some fronts, but this is decreasing), and that we can often move things into NetSuite instead. This lightens the work and financial burden required to have the capabilities our business needs. We can do many things with one NetSuite developer that would take a whole team and separate software suite to do otherwise.

While NetSuite doesn't excel in every area, having capability in almost every area makes it possible for us to run things in a more lightweight way, and pivot as our business changes. Super-flexible but sometimes slow, and

column7: It is a really powerful tool, the built-in reporting is great. Everything lives in the same ecosystem, making it easy to cross reference information.
column8: There is a really steep learning curve to get understand how the program works. The implementation team focuses on getting your data into the system, then you're on your own to figure out how it actually works. Saved searches seem to be crucial, but building them is not intuitive at all.
